item.title: Hormuz | Tokyo urges Tehran to cease actions that threaten navigation
item.description: Tokyo on Tuesday urged Tehran to cease actions that threaten navigation in the Strait of Hormuz, as the debate intensifies over international intervention to protect maritime traffic in that fundamental route for energy trade. Japanese Foreign Minister Toshimitsu Motegi made the appeal on Tuesday night during a phone call with his Iranian counterpart, Abbas Araghchi, according to a statement from the head of Japanese diplomacy. “Minister Motegi strongly urged Iran to immediately cease attacks on civilian installations and infrastructure in the Gulf countries, as well as actions that threaten the safety of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz,” the statement revealed. The Japanese minister expressed deep concern about the continuation of the exchange of “retaliatory attacks” since the previous telephone call held on March 9, and with the extent of the damage, which even affected neighboring countries. Furthermore, Motegi expressed concern about the high number of ships linked to Japan that are currently stranded in the Persian Gulf and asked Iran to take the necessary measures to guarantee the safety of all ships in the Strait of Hormuz, “including those from Japan and other Asian countries”. For his part, Araghchi explained Iran's position and both ministers agreed to maintain close communication with a view to quickly easing tensions, according to the Japanese statement. Expectations of a potential deployment of Japanese military ships to the region could be one of the key themes at the summit this Thursday in Washington between Japanese Prime Minister, Sanae Takaichi, and the US President. Asked yesterday about the possibility of sending Japanese Self-Defense Forces personnel to the area, Takaichi reiterated that, for now, there are no plans to do so.

item.title: Natural Disasters | Chan La Kei calls for more extreme exercises
item.description: Representative Chan La Kei wants to know whether security forces will carry out more extreme simulations to respond to natural disasters and bad weather. The matter is addressed through a written interpellation, published on the Legislative Assembly portal linked to the Fujian community. “Will the authorities create more extreme simulated scenarios, taking into account the weaknesses detected last year, such as ‘consecutive occurrence of tropical storms in Macau, causing pressure on respective resources’, ‘obstacles in cross-border material support’, etc., in order to assess the interdepartmental and interregional dynamic response and prevention capacity?”, asked the legislator. In the same vein, Chan La Kei questions the authorities about plans to create localized alerts for different areas of the territory, depending on the level of impact of natural occurrences, as well as the possibility of using artificial intelligence to produce cell phone messages in several languages ​​to alert those affected. The deputy highlights that the use of several languages ​​in communication is important, because responding to an emergency situation often requires not wasting time. Among the issues raised about emergency response, the deputy also wants to know how authorities are currently able to identify places where unforeseen situations occur and how they can speed up response mechanisms to promptly send security agents to the location.

item.title: Firefighters | Almost 6 thousand inspections of residential buildings
item.description: In 2025, the Fire Department carried out almost 6,000 fire safety inspections of local buildings. The number was revealed through the response to a question from deputy Ho Ion Sang “regarding the issue of misuse of common areas for individual purposes by condominiums. Throughout 2025, the Fire Department (CB) carried out a total of 11,760 fire safety inspections and checks, including 5,957 inspections in residential buildings”, it was communicated, in a response signed by the president of the Housing Institute (IH), Iam Lei Leng. “In addition to carrying out inspections in accordance with previously defined plans, the CB also responds to requests from managers, building managers or competent departments, or when it receives complaints about impacts on fire safety, sending personnel to the site to carry out verification and appropriate treatment”, it was added. In the response, it was not stated how many requests for correction of the detected situations were made or whether fines or other sanctions were applied. In relation to the environment in community neighborhoods, and the abandonment of garbage or dripping water from the air conditioning, according to the Institute of Municipal Affairs, “more than 14 thousand accusations” were registered for infractions. “In 2025, the IAM applied more than 14 thousand charges in relation to the situations mentioned”, it was indicated.

item.title: FRC | Poetry and Reading Featured Today
item.description: The Rui Cunha Foundation (FRC) presents today, starting at 6:30 pm, the session “An Encounter of Poetry: The Tree and everything they want”, which aims to celebrate World Poetry Day, a date established by UNESCO at the 30th General Conference in Paris, in 1999. The event also marks the International Day of Forests, with both dates to be marked on March 21st by the United Nations. Today's session is organized by the Reading Club of the Associação dos Amigos do Livro in Macau, and, as described by the FRC in a statement, “an opportunity to encourage reading, support poets and preserve linguistic and cultural diversity, through the written and spoken word”. The session, to be led by doctor and writer Shee Vá, aims to encourage the public to share poems that have a special meaning in their lives, which may or may not be subject to the “Tree” theme. What matters is “honoring poetic expression and its cultural importance as an identity legacy of the language of each country or countries”. "Poetry, with its ability to move, provoke reflection and transmit feelings, has been able to cross centuries and civilizations, being one of the pillars of literary art. If you want to participate, show up at the FRC Gallery and ask to speak. Come prepared to listen and read, contributing to enriching the experience of local communities in this aim of giving voice to the feelings of poets and touching souls", describes the same note about the event.

item.title: Excursion | Forced purchases investigated in Macau, HK and Zhuhai
item.description: Authorities in Shenzhen are investigating a case of forced purchases that allegedly occurred on a tour of Macau, Zhuhai and Hong Kong. The situation was revealed by the Chinese newspaper Jimu News, which infiltrated the low-cost tour, which will have cost tourists 380 yuan for a five-day trip to the three cities, between the 8th and 12th of March. The report revealed forced purchasing practices and constant pressure to purchase products from selected stores on the tour organized by the Yantian (Shenzhen) district branch of Hengsheng International Travel Agency. The low-cost trip attracted mostly elderly people who ended up spending most of their time in outlet stores, where store security guards prevented them from leaving until they had permission from the tour guide. The newspaper reports that elderly people ended up being coerced into buying medicines worth thousands of yuan, including three “middle-aged” people who reportedly spent around 10,000 yuan in a single day. When expenses fell short of expectations, the guide reacted with anger and shouting, threatening to continue visiting stores until the purchases satisfied the merchants involved in the scheme. The guide even threatened to cut off meals to two excursionists who, after spending more than 1,000 yuan each, asked that the focus of the tour be more on points of tourist interest instead of shops. The culture and tourism department of Yantian district was visited by authorities who initiated mediation procedures between injured parties and the travel agency.

item.title: Jardim Nova Taipa | Fire sends man to hospital
item.description: A man was found on the floor where the fire broke out with second-degree burns to his face, hands and feet. He was transported to the hospital in “stable” condition. The flames started in an apartment that was being renovated A 65-year-old man had to be hospitalized yesterday after suffering burns due to a fire in Urbanização Jardim Nova Taipa. According to information from the Fire Department (CB), the flames broke out yesterday morning, around 9 am, in an apartment that was being renovated, and the injured person was one of the workers on site. When rescue teams reached the 24th floor, where the fire originated, they found the man on the floor, with second-degree burns on his face, hands and feet. The worker was removed from the building by rescue teams and transported to the Centro Hospitalar Conde de São Januário, where authorities indicated that he is in a stable condition and far from danger. It was also reported that several residents received treatment in ambulances at the scene. In the apartment where the fire started, and which was being renovated, firefighters came across open flames that were extinguished using water hoses. Furthermore, to speed up the removal of smoke from inside the apartment, rescue teams used extraction fans. According to the newspaper Ou Mun, the rescued man recognized that before the explosion there had been a gas leak. However, the space was ventilated and work continued. The explosion occurred when a mower created a spark that led to the explosion. Run for life According to the Chinese channel Radio Macau, when residents of the building noticed the flames they began to abandon their apartments, which led to 50 people leaving on their own. Speaking to the newspaper Ou Mun, some residents who were outside the building, while firefighters were fighting the fire, said they heard a large explosion and felt the building shaking. After that moment, they saw flames coming from inside the affected apartment. Also a resident with the surname Ho, who lives close to the affected building, stated that he had returned home when the explosion was heard. He went out into the street to see what was going on and noticed that there were pieces of glass on the sidewalk. Due to the fight against the flames, several sections of Rua de Bragança and Rua de Foshan were temporarily closed to traffic. In reaction to the fire, the Institute of Social Action (IAS) stated that it was monitoring the situation and providing accommodation for affected residents. “The Social Action Institute is deeply concerned about the fire that occurred at Urbanização Jardim Nova Taipa, in Taipa”, it was announced. “As soon as the IAS became aware of the incident, it coordinated with social service agencies in Taipa to send teams to the scene to provide support and comfort to the affected residents,” it added. According to yesterday morning's statement, there had been no request for temporary accommodation.

item.title: Flu | Number of children who went to hospital almost doubles
item.description: The Health Services launched an alert for the increase in flu cases, with the rate of positive tests for the influenza virus reaching almost 18 percent, exceeding the threshold that requires an alert to be issued. Authorities call for vaccination, especially pregnant women, children and the elderly The Health Services issued an alert on Tuesday regarding the increase in flu cases in Macau. “Laboratory results show that the positivity rate in influenza virus tests gradually increased from 5 percent in the week of February 22 to 28 to 17.8 percent last week (March 8 to 14), exceeding the alert level of 15.2 percent,” the authorities specify. More than 70 percent of these cases involved the influenza B virus, which represents an evolution compared to previous periods of high influenza prevalence. The two previous peak phases of the influenza virus in Macau were between mid-November 2025 and early January 2026, with the H3 subtype of influenza A virus being the predominant strain in circulation. According to data from the emergency services of the three hospitals in the region, there was a significant increase in consultations for children due to flu-like symptoms. The number of consultations gradually increased from the end of February, rising from 177 per week between 22 and 28 February, to 342 per week last week (8 to 14 March). The Health Services emphasize that the volume of adult consultations due to similar symptoms has remained “relatively stable”. Better to prevent With the virus returning to a higher level of activity, the Health Services appealed to the population to take precautions, especially pregnant women, young children, the elderly and people with chronic illnesses who have not yet been vaccinated against the flu. The authorities reinforced that these demographic groups “are more likely to suffer serious complications or even die, so they should be vaccinated as quickly as possible”. Authorities revealed on Tuesday that five collective cases of flu affected schools in the territory, with infections exceeding fifty. Regarding these types of outbreaks in kindergartens and schools, the most recent cases are mainly caused by the influenza B virus. “Cases gradually increased from mid-February, going from zero in the first three weeks to 10 last week, with nine cases registered in the first three days of this week”, indicated the Health Services, noting that there have been no serious cases of flu since January.

item.title: Courts | Portugal opens two judge vacancies for Macau
item.description: After the president of the Superior Council of the Judiciary (CSM) expressed openness to sending more judges to Macau in October last year, the Portuguese authorities have now opened a recruitment competition The Superior Council of the Judiciary (CSM) of Portugal announced on Tuesday the opening of recruitment of two Portuguese judges for positions in Macau's first instance courts in the civil area. According to the published notice, judicial magistrates with a seniority of more than seven years and less than 15 years can apply, with the appointment being valid for two years, possibly renewable. Applications must be submitted by March 31, and according to the document prepared by the Independent Commission Responsible for the Appointment of Judges of the Macao SAR, the selected magistrates will have a salary and benefits equivalent to those of local judges of the same category and seniority. The salary corresponds to a percentage of the Chief Executive's monthly salary, currently set at 268,297 patacas, which varies according to the length of service as a judge, between 50 percent and 60 percent of the monthly amount earned by the Government leader. In addition to remuneration, judges will be entitled to holiday and Christmas allowances, housing or rental and equipment allowances, medical assistance for themselves and their household, as well as judicial holidays of 22 working days per year. Portugal and Macau maintain a judicial cooperation agreement that ensures the continuity of Portuguese magistrates (judges and prosecutors) in the territory, supporting the Portuguese-based legal system. This collaboration focuses on recruitment, training and the exchange of judicial experiences. Bridging exits The only judge from Portugal currently working in Macau is Jerónimo Alberto Gonçalves Santos, judge of the Court of Second Instance, after judge Rui Ribeiro brought forward the end of the special commission, which ended in May 2026, to the end of October 2025. In 2024, the CSM rejected the stay of Portuguese judge of the Court of First Instance Carlos Carvalho, who had been in Macau for 16 years and had been invited by the Independent Commission for the Appointment of Judges in the territory to continue for another two years. Despite the rejection, in October last year, during a visit to Macau, the leader of the Supreme Court of Justice, João Cura Mariano, who inherently also presides over the CSM, told journalists that if the Macau authorities showed interest there would be an opening to send more Portuguese magistrates to the territory. “We are going to try to see what is really useful that they can still convey and whether they are necessary, because if they are necessary, new judges will come”, he guaranteed.

item.title: Tobacco | Government finds it difficult to ban smoking from those walking on sidewalks
item.description: At a time when public consultation is taking place on increasing restrictions on tobacco consumption, the Government is ruling out the possibility of a total ban on smoking and increasing taxes on tobacco The head of the Health Services (SS) Smoking and Alcoholism Prevention and Control Office, Lam Chong, considers it difficult to implement a smoking ban for those walking on the streets. The limitation was recognized during the morning program Forum Macau on the Chinese channel Radio Macau. “We studied practices worldwide regarding people smoking while walking and no country or region has managed to implement this type of ban,” said Lam Chong, after being confronted by a listener who called the program to defend the ban. “The most applicable practices, which are also foreseen in the proposed changes, include classifying areas with the highest flow of visitors as places where smoking is prohibited”, he indicated. Lam Chong acknowledged that the measures do not satisfy the desire of part of the population to prohibit those who walk from smoking. However, there is hope among the Government to help reduce the impact of smoking in outdoor places with greater concentrations of people. At the same time, Lam Chong highlighted that the classification of certain areas as places where smoking is prohibited can become a message to smokers that their addiction is seen as an undesirable act. Anti-tobacco fight In yesterday's Forum Macau program, changes to the tobacco prevention and control regime were discussed, as a public consultation is taking place until 8 April. According to the measures proposed by the Executive, a total ban on any contact with electronic cigarettes, as well as other tobacco products, such as pee, will be implemented. Previously, the Executive explained this option by considering that these types of tobacco products are gateways to the world of drugs. However, the Executive will continue to ban traditional cigarettes, even though yesterday some listeners advocated a ban on all tobacco products or a huge increase in tobacco tax. However, Lam Chong defended traditional cigarettes: “A total ban is not appropriate, because people will smoke in secret”, he stressed. Currently, tobacco tax takes up 60 percent of the retail price. Lam Chong argued that the price should not be increased further through taxes, because the value is lower in neighboring regions, which will lead to more and more smuggling. No magazines Regarding the ban on the possession of electronic cigarettes in public places, Lam Chong pointed out that it will target all smokers, even those who only have this type of equipment stored in a bag. However, the head of the Office for the Prevention and Control of Smoking and Alcoholism indicated that inspectors will not be competent to carry out searches, therefore, situations in which cigarettes are hidden should not lead to fines. Another listener expressed concern that the video recording by inspectors may violate personal privacy. In response, SS deputy director Cheang Seng Ip assured that inspectors only turn on video cameras if there is a conflict and threats during the law enforcement process. The person in charge also explained that before turning on the recording equipment, inspectors will have to inform the individuals involved. And if there were no abnormal occurrences, the recording will be deleted after seven days.

item.title: China/Guinea-Bissau | A relationship that began with decolonization
item.description: In “Relations between the People's Republic of China and Guinea-Bissau: between diplomacy, economy and security”, academic Orazio Maria Gnerre describes a path that began with Guinea-Bissau's independence. The article, published in the academic journal Janus, speaks of mutual interests that allow “the near future of Guinea-Bissau and China” to remain “intimately linked” At a time when China is seeking to cement relations with the so-called “Global South”, namely Africa and Latin America, commercial and diplomatic links with Portuguese-speaking African countries have been increasing. Guinea-Bissau, a country that has been experiencing internal political tensions, has been one of China's African partners. The relationship was analyzed by academic Orazio Maria Gnerre in the article “Relations between the People's Republic of China and Guinea-Bissau: between diplomacy, economy and security”, published in the magazine Janus – E-Journal of International Relations, from the Universidade Autónoma de Lisboa. The author describes how the relationship between the two countries began in 1975, when Guinea-Bissau gained independence from Portugal, after 13 years of Colonial War, and how these connections to the People's Republic of China (PRC) are far from over, taking into account the existence of mutual economic interests, especially because “the PRC outlined an important investment plan in Guinea-Bissau”. The author concludes that “relations between China and Guinea-Bissau”, cemented “in a long period of contemporary history, from the context of the Cold War to the present day, in a phase of increasing multipolarization”, occurred “in a double way”. In other words, there has been “a diplomatic dialogue permitted by a certain type of political-cultural attitude and by a realistic approach in relation to the economic and commercial needs of the two countries”. In this relationship between states, “the colonial issue is not of secondary importance”, taking into account that the decolonization process provided “a framework of political significance for cooperation between the two countries (which, therefore, also occurs in areas beyond the merely economic)”, in addition to having guaranteed “the development of a certain type of international trade”. In the Portuguese decolonization process, 51 years ago, “China entered the political game of Portuguese-speaking nations and those that were under Portuguese rule, including Guinea-Bissau”, having started to develop during this period “the idea of ​​South-South trade”, launched at the Bandung Conference in 1955. It is important to emphasize that, in these years after decolonization, Guinea-Bissau broke relations with the PRC to strengthen ties with Taiwan, something that ended in 1998. In these years in which China had a relationship with the African country, “economic exchanges between the two countries” took place, with the PRC “financing the construction of a stadium, a hospital and other facilities”. More recently, Guinea-Bissau joined the “Belt and Road” initiative, having signed, in 2021, a memorandum of understanding with China. "This type of project could certainly provide Guinea-Bissau with some advantages, if it were fully used to increase investments for development. Furthermore, guaranteeing the construction of infrastructure projects, such as ports, highways and railways, is of great importance for Sub-Saharan African countries", he analyzed. One of the most recent agreements focuses on the mining sector, with “an important agreement” having been signed with Chinalco. “The partnership (with the Belt and Road Initiative project) manifests itself not only in the construction of infrastructure, but in commercial alliances”. Not everything is roses Orazio Maria Gnerre considers that “the near future of Guinea-Bissau and China appears to be closely linked”, even though there have been “ups and downs” in this relationship. The volatility focuses on “the difficult period” experienced by Guinea-Bissau “in terms of growth and development”. "Political tensions and the impact of natural disasters make it difficult to build solid international relations that allow the country to develop better infrastructure and assert itself both in the region and globally. The complex relations between the People's Republic of China and the country also fit into this context." Therefore, “this element must be taken into account, considering the important presence of the Asian country on the African continent, as well as the business volume and support for the construction of infrastructures that it guarantees to many African nations”. It is described that, in this context, “diplomatic relations between the African country and its political parties with China have been fluctuating and marked by internal issues, as well as international events”. Today the African continent “is growing, both economically and in terms of infrastructure” and Guinea-Bissau also fits into this growth, being a country that, after 1975, “was initially led by the authority of the national liberation movement and, subsequently, entered a phase of serious political confrontations and relative instability”. It is, however, a nation with “a lot of resources and a great potential for growth that must only be used in the best possible way”, which is why the Guinean authorities seek for the country to grow “in the field of infrastructure and build its own economic-political autonomy based on the sectors in which it finds the greatest advantages”. Thus, “it is in this context that we can insert and interpret the relations between the sub-Saharan country and the People's Republic of China”. In its history, Guinea-Bissau “received help from the Asian country [China] in this delicate process [of decolonization], which favored alternating diplomatic relations between these two political entities”. According to the author, “the theoretical profile of Chinese politics has always addressed both the group of countries emerging from colonization, and the idea of ​​cooperation for development between decolonized countries”, which is why “the relations between Guinea-Bissau and China are so important and so strongly characterized in a political sense”. Internationalization is the key The author and academic highlights “two trends that come together almost spontaneously” in the relationship between China and Africa. “On the one hand, China seeks to establish partnerships with African countries, both to follow the premises of its international economic policy — formulated since the beginnings of the revolutionary communist government — and to acquire, through trade and cooperation, raw materials of great importance for the country's industry.” Then there is “the attempt by many African countries to grow and guarantee more and more stability and security”, with “internationalization, access to international markets and the sale of their raw materials becoming a necessity for these countries”. Internationalization, through trade and export of products, is “of vital importance, as is attracting foreign investment”. Thus, the academic states that the political history of Guinea-Bissau determined all these political and economic trends that, to date, are relevant within the scope of a complex analysis of the country's current situation, as well as its specific relationships. “The real weight that the phenomenon of decolonization had conditioned, in all aspects, the form that the country, its trade and its diplomatic action took”, creating a scenario of comparison “with contemporary China, also born from a phenomenon of decolonization”, despite the necessary historical and contextual differences. This panorama ended up creating, between the two countries, “a certain type of economic-political relationship that intervenes in a context that, without a doubt, is not easy (both at a regional and global level), but which is advancing at an accelerated pace in a global situation undergoing complete restructuring”. The article also cites data that show how Guinea-Bissau has registered some development, despite going through “alternate phases of success in terms of economic growth”, thanks to great volatility “due to events of a political or environmental nature”. Furthermore, the author recalls that Guinea-Bissau “is frequently hit by climate shocks”, and in 2003, 2004 and 2005 it suffered “serious floods that damaged its infrastructure, villages and the agricultural sector, leaving a serious impact on the economy and society”.

item.title: Hormuz | Tokyo urges Tehran to cease actions that threaten navigation
item.description: Tokyo on Tuesday urged Tehran to cease actions that threaten navigation in the Strait of Hormuz, as the debate intensifies over international intervention to protect maritime traffic in that fundamental route for energy trade. Japanese Foreign Minister Toshimitsu Motegi made the appeal on Tuesday night during a phone call with his Iranian counterpart, Abbas Araghchi, according to a statement from the head of Japanese diplomacy. “Minister Motegi strongly urged Iran to immediately cease attacks on civilian installations and infrastructure in the Gulf countries, as well as actions that threaten the safety of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz,” the statement revealed. Furthermore, Motegi expressed concern about the high number of ships linked to Japan that are currently stranded in the Persian Gulf and asked Iran to take the necessary measures to guarantee the safety of all ships in the Strait of Hormuz, “including those from Japan and other Asian countries”. For his part, Araghchi explained Iran's position and both ministers agreed to maintain close communication with a view to quickly easing tensions, according to the Japanese statement. Expectations of a potential deployment of Japanese military ships to the region could be one of the key themes at the summit this Thursday in Washington between Japanese Prime Minister, Sanae Takaichi, and the US President. Asked yesterday about the possibility of sending Japanese Self-Defense Forces personnel to the area, Takaichi reiterated that, for now, there are no plans to do so.

item.title: Cinema | Four Portuguese co-productions highlighted at the Hong Kong festival
item.description: Four Portuguese co-productions will be part of the lineup at the Hong Kong International Film Festival, which will take place between the 1st and 12th of April. “O Riso e a Faca” by Pedro Pinho, “Magalhães” by Lav Diaz, “Amílcar” by Miguel Eek and “Last night I conquered the city of Thebes” by Gabriel Azorín join Manoel de Oliveira’s classic “Aniki-Bóbó” According to the schedule of the Hong Kong International Film Festival (HKIFF), released on Tuesday, the World Cinema category includes the film “The Laughter and the Knife”, the second fiction feature film by Portuguese director Pedro Pinho. The co-production between Portugal, Brazil, France and Romania was a candidate for the Goya 2026 – Spanish film awards – for best European film and for the 2026 European film awards from the European Film Academy. “The Laughter and the Knife”, by Pedro Pinho, with Sérgio Coragem, Cléo Diára and Jonathan Guilherme in the cast, premiered in May at the Cannes Film Festival, in France, earning the actress a representation award in the “Un Certain Regard” section. In the Masters and Authors section, “Magalhães”, by Filipino director Lav Diaz, will be shown, about the Portuguese navigator Fernão de Magalhães, who was the Philippines' candidate for an Oscar nomination for Best International Film. The film, starring Mexican actor Gael García Bernal as the Portuguese navigator Ferdinand Magellan (1480-1521), who embarked on a circumnavigation journey and died in the Philippines. “Magalhães”, with Portuguese co-production by Rosa Filmes, also features actress Ângela Machado, in the role of Beatriz Barbosa, the navigator's wife, and actors Tomás Alves, Rafael Morais, Ivo Arroja and Valdemar Santos. In the company of masters In the same section, there is the most recent documentary by German Werner Herzog, “Ghost Elephants”, recorded in Angola, and which premiered at the 82nd edition of the Venice International Film Festival. Among the documentaries to be shown at HKIFF is “Amílcar”, which is the result of a five-year work around the independent African politician Amílcar Cabral, made by Spanish director Miguel Eek. The co-production between Spain, France, Switzerland, Cape Verde and Portugal, by Lx Films, has photography direction by João Pedro Plácido, and is nominated for the award for best Ibero-American documentary at the Guadalajara Film Festival, which will take place in April. In the Kaleidoscope – Poesia em Movimento section of the HUFF will be “Last night I conquered the city of Thebes”, by Gabriel Azorín, a Spanish production with the Portuguese First Age. The festival will also present several classic films restored in 4K, including “Aniki-Bóbó” (1942), the first film by Portuguese director Manoel de Oliveira (1908-2015). The HKIFF includes a competition for short films, the Firebirds awards, and one of the nominees is “Samba Infinito”, a co-production between Brazil and France, directed by Brazilian Leonardo Martinelli.

item.title: Tourism | Number of Chinese traveling to Japan fell by 45%
item.description: The number of Chinese tourists in Japan fell 45.2 percent in February, compared to February 2025, despite the Lunar New Year holidays, according to official data published yesterday, in a context of strong tensions between Tokyo and Beijing. The archipelago received 396,400 visitors from mainland China last month, according to the Japanese Tourism Agency, despite the supposedly prosperous Chinese New Year period, which this year fell in February. China is, by far, the largest tourist source market for Japan, representing approximately a quarter of all arrivals in the year until the end of November, but with significant successive drops since then. Relations between the two countries have deteriorated since Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi's statements about Taiwan in the Japanese parliament last November, following which Beijing urged its citizens to avoid any trips to Japan. Takaichi suggested at the time that the country's military intervention would be possible in the event of a Chinese attack on Taiwan, an island that Beijing considers inalienable Chinese territory. In February it announced that it would sanction 40 Japanese companies and organizations accused of participating in the new militarization of Japan, namely prohibiting 20 of them from acquiring goods and technologies with both civil and military potential from companies based in China.

item.title: Studio City | Countdown is now called REM
item.description: Concessionaire Melco Resorts & Entertainment announced that it will change the name of the Countdown hotel to REM. Publicity was made through social media. The name change will happen when the ongoing renovation work is completed, which is expected by the end of this year. In August last year, the concessionaire announced plans to transform the hotel's 330 rooms into 150 suites for the luxury segment. In addition to the Studio City entertainment complex, the concessionaire that belongs to Lawrence Ho, son of tycoon Stanley Ho, is also responsible for the City of Dreams and Altira tourist developments. The company also has investments in the Philippines and Cyprus.

item.title: Ana Cristina Alves
item.description:
Ana Cristina Alves – Assistant Researcher and Coordinator of the Educational Service of the Macau Scientific and Cultural Center The popular myth of origin “Pangu Separates the Heaven” (盘古开天Pángǔ kāi tiān) dates back to the Three Kingdoms Period (三国, 220-280) and its Three-Five Calendar Record (《三五历纪》)2, according to data from the online encyclopedia Baike.Baidu, where the Hun Tian cosmological theory (“浑天说”) is exposed, which means “theory of the Celestial Globe” or, in a less literal translation, “theory of the cosmic egg””3: The Three Five Calendar Record reads "Heaven and Earth were linked like an egg, giving rise to Pangu. In Hun Tian theory, the celestial sphere surrounds the earth, and the center where Heaven and Earth meet is known as 'the geographic center'. It was in Luoyang that Pangu was born, at that time Heaven and Earth had not yet separated, the universe was a chaotic mixture. Pangu slept in this chaos for 18 thousand years. One day, suddenly He woke up. All around him, there was pitch darkness. He raised a large ax with which he fought against the darkness. It dissipated with an immense noise and gradually became the Earth. After Heaven and Earth had separated, the titan feared that they would unite again, so that this would never be possible, he placed his head against Heaven, leaving his feet firmly planted on Earth. And that's how Heaven rose upwards, moving three meters away from Earth every day. For many years he too followed the growth of Heaven, ever higher and more distant. When Heaven and Earth were fully formed, Pangu fell exhausted. After his fall, his body underwent the greatest changes: His breath became the wind and clouds of the four seasons; the voice in thunderous thunder; eyes on the sun and moon, the four cardinal points of the Earth, east, west, south and north; the muscles and skin across the vast expanse of the Earth; the blood in small and large rivers always flowing and the sweat, in the rain and dew that nourish the ten thousand beings. 据《三五历纪》记载，“天地浑沌如鸡子，盘古生其中” 。浑天说中，球壳状的天包着地，天与地的中心就是“地中”，即洛阳，盘古生于此。当时天和地还没有分开，宇宙一片。盘古在这个混沌的宇宙中睡了一万八千年。有一天，盘古突然醒了。他见周围一片漆黑，他就抡起大斧头，朝眼前的黑暗猛劈过去了。只听一声巨响，一片黑暗的东西渐渐分散开了。轻清的东西缓缓上升，变成了天；重浊的东西慢慢下降，变成了地。 天和地分开以后，盘古怕它们还会合在一起，便头顶着天，脚蹬着地。天每天升高一丈，盘古也随着天越长越高。这样不知过多少年，天和地逐渐成形了，盘古也累得倒下来了。 盘古倒下后，他呼出的气息，变成了四季的风和云；他发出的声音，化作了隆隆的雷声:的四肢, Article
João Marcelo Martins in A Gramática Universal do Mito (2023, 142) draws attention to the various etymological meanings of the name of Pangu (盘古), which, in a possible reading, is close to the phonetics of the word «cabaça», Páohù (匏瓠), which in Chinese culture has the symbolic meaning of reproduction of life, there are still other interpretative possibilities surrounding the name of this great titan, such as that of a «spiral antiquity» (Ibidem), because one of the sinograms is 古 (gǔ), which means «ancient», while the other 盘 (pán), can be translated as «spiral». The truth is that this Chinese myth of the cosmic egg is found in several mythologies, such as the Orphic mythology of ancient Greece, which emphasizes the image of a large egg from which all the beings of the universe sprouted, like chicks, including the very bases of it – Heaven and Earth. An identical panorama presents us with the Chinese genesis myth. In this creation myth, we are given an image that appeals to a chaotic mixture, in which the universe is immersed in total darkness. Afterwards, we witness the sacrifice of the titan, who manages to separate and constitute the two basic forces, representatives of the masculine principle Yang (阳), Heaven, and the feminine principle Yin (阴), the earth. All of creation is an act of sacrifice of the portentous, which begins in the thousands of years in which the divine force separates the elements and culminates in the exhaustion of the titan. It is from Pangu's body and, through transformation, that all living beings, celestial and telluric, emerge. There is an immanent, living and animated energy that keeps a universe connected, as if in one body. The generous sacrifices don’t stop there. Neither in China, nor in ancient Greece, do they become very clear when one thinks about the descriptions found for the creation of humanity by the Goddess Nϋwa (女娲), that in some version of the myth “Nϋwa creates human beings” (女娲造人Nǚ wā zào rén) after creating humanity with the help of the earth and filling it with people, she falls deeply asleep, such was the state of tiredness and one is left without knowing if he will not have woken up in paradise never to return to the world. The Goddess, after creating the animals, continued to feel alone, so "on the seventh day, she arrived at a lake. She took some clay and mixed it with water. When she saw her reflected image, she made a piece of clay similar to her" (Wang, Alves, 2009, 16). To make it move, it was enough to place the doll and all the others that would follow, in contact with the earth. The energy of this animated them, being in itself indispensable in the transition from dolls to the creation of humanity. As happened in the ancient Greek world, the cosmos arises from the void and primordial Chaos, then, through spontaneous generation, as stated in Hesiod's theogony, Mother-Earth Gaia, Eros, the God of Love, manifests itself and from this all the deified elements: the Sky or Uranus, the Sea and the Mountains, etc. Gaia, with the help of Uranus, generates the remaining children, some of them colossal and deformed creatures like the Cyclops, with fifty heads and one hundred arms, in addition to the titans, the giants, as well as the remaining gods, which Cronus, the god of time called titans, which meant in his idea “arrogant and ambitious creatures”. The Greek Gods had all human defects and virtues, they were evil, jealous, vengeful, but also kind and giving, depending on the way they behaved on their moods. In ancient Greece, humanity was not forged in the likeness of the gods, but, on the contrary, the gods were conceived in the image and likeness of humanity. Poor those divine forces that out of love for humanity tried to deceive their peers, as happened with the titan Prometheus who went to Olympus to steal fire from the gods to provide humanity with a powerful weapon. The episode took place after Epimetheus, Prometheus' younger brother, whose name means “what he thinks after”, endowed the animals, when he created them with the best virtues that he took from the saddlebag prepared by the younger gods: claws, fangs, wings, tentacles, beaks, stingers, speed and everything else that animals have. Prometheus, whose name means “foresighted thinker”, upon concluding that there was nothing left for himself on earth that would distinguish humanity, went to Olympus, deceiving and extorting the fire of the gods, thus endowing human beings with intelligence so that they could defend themselves from animals with physical capabilities far superior to theirs. To do so, he had to catch Hestia, the virgin goddess of the home, distracted, but the same did not happen to Zeus, the supreme leader of the gods, who was not fooled. He punished what he considered to be the supreme arrogance of the titan thief with a terrible torture. He shackled Prometheus in the Caucasus Mountains and ordered Hephaestus, god of forging and other arts, to make shackles to chain him in his furnace. The creator of humanity was trapped by Power and Force on a cliff, with arms and legs stretched out, held by the wrists and ankles, being visited daily by a voracious eagle, which tore apart his diaphragm and ate his liver. Aeschylus (525- 456 BC), the great Greek playwright, recounts the torture, inspired by Hesiod's Theogony, in the tragedy Prometheus Bound, leaving the hero to unburden himself at the end of the play that recounts his adventure in the gift of fire to Humanity: (…) Here’s the gust who, to bring terror upon me, It was sent by Zeus. Oh Majesty! Oh my mother! Divine ether! ― That, across the world beyond, phases roll wonderful hymn: the Light – supreme good, to illuminate the tragic fate from everyone! See how great my misfortune is, The pain that tortures me - Unfairly!… (Aeschylus, 1946, 115) Prometheus' sacrifice will have an echo in the Christian world, in which the son of God, Jesus Christ, also sacrifices himself for the love of Humanity. He dies on the cross to save men, who invariably behave badly. But there is a big difference in the way Chinese and Christians view the cosmos, with ancient Greece establishing the bridge between both worldviews. From the Christian perspective, God created the world out of nothing (Creatio ex nihilo). It is a transcendent, personal creation with the artist's signature. The fruit of creation may not have been the best, but it will always remain unexplained how evil entered the world, even with Saint Augustine defending the theory of human free will. Here is the account of creation ex nihilo in the book of Genesis of the Old Testament (Gen 1): In the beginning God created Heaven and Earth. The Earth was formless and empty; darkness covered the abyss and a fierce wind blew over the waters. God said: “Let there be light!” And the light began to exist. God saw that the light was good. God separated the light from the darkness: the light God called “day” and the darkness he called “night”. There was an afternoon and a morning, it was the first day. Then followed the creation of the firmament, which God called heaven on the second day. On the third day, he created the earth, the sea and the plants. On the fourth day the sun, moon and stars were created. On the fifth day, the fish, the birds and all living beings. On the sixth day, animals were divided into species and man was created. On the seventh day he rested. Regarding the creation of humanity, God in one version creates them directly and in his image and likeness (Gn 1, 27-31), in the other he creates first the man and then the woman from his rib (Gn 2,18-23). After which, a long road is covered until we reach the New Testament and the sacrifice of Jesus Christ, who died on the cross for the love of humanity. There is a big difference, from a philosophical point of view, between the Chinese and Greek immanentist cosmologies and the Christian transcendent one. The first obey the principle of transformation of elements that have always been immersed in primordial chaos and, therefore, present an interrelated cosmological image, in which the elements or ten thousand beings have root affinities that allow them to communicate and transmute themselves whenever necessary. Greek gods, for example, take whatever forms they wish, just like Chinese deities and immortals. In the Christian West, the absence of a cosmic egg or primordial chaos hinders and prevents transmutation. Beings are all separate and in an external position in relation to divinity, since if one is made in the image of god, one is not the divine himself and to achieve this status a mystical union with him is supposed. The profane exterior, split, distant will have to travel a long path, which perhaps involves martyrdom and sacrifice already experienced by the son of God. In any case, even within the scope of immanentist philosophies, guided by the principle of transformation, sacrifice is also always present, when combined with love for humanity, as in the case of Prometheus or, in part, of Nϋwa; or without love, but by natural essence, when the alliance is made over time, if the example of Pangu is considered. The gods are sacrificed, but they are not alone. The sacrifice is universal, because natural creatures are also martyred for love or time in the three cosmogonies presented, they immolate themselves to deities, some more distant, others so close that they can only be intuited from within. References Holy Bible. 1997. São Paulo: Paulus. Squirrel. 1946. Prometheus Fettered. Translated by Eduardo Scarlatti. Lisbon: Livraria Luso-Espanhola, Lda “浑天说” (Hun Tian theory) In Baidu. Baike. https://baike.baidu.com/item/%E6%B5%91%E5%A4%A9%E8%AF%B4/717090?fromModule=lemma_inlink, accessed February 25, 2026. “盘古开天” (Pangu separates Heaven). In Baidu. Baike. https://baike.baidu.com/item/%E7%9B%98%E5%8F%A4%E5%BC%80%E5%A4%A9/74007，2008年6月7日, accessed February 24, 2026. Johnston, Sarah Iles. 2025. Gods and Mortals. Odivelas: Soul of Books Martins, João Cancelo. 2023. The Universal Grammar of Myth: Comparative and Contrastive Analysis of Chinese Myths of Origin. Lisbon: Macau Scientific and Cultural Center, University of Macau. Wang Suoying, Ana Cristina Alves. 2009. Myths and Legends of the land of the Dragon. Lisbon: Path. Notes: I This space has the collaboration of the Macau Scientific and Cultural Center, in Lisbon, and the opinions expressed in the article are the sole responsibility of the authors. According to scholar João Martins in The Universal Grammar of Myth. Comparative and Contrastive Analysis of Chinese Origin Myths (2023, 143, note 100) The Three Five Calendar concerns the now lost historical records of the Three Sovereign Deities and the Five Gods. iii The Hun Tian theory, founded by Zhang Heng (张衡), an astronomer from the Han Dynasty, is an astronomical theory between the geocentric and heliocentric theses, in which it is argued that the universe is infinite, the movement of celestial bodies is regular, moonlight is the reflection of sunlight and lunar eclipses are caused by the Earth blocking sunlight. To prove his Hun Tian Theory, Zhang Heng built the first “water-powered armillary sphere”, ushering in an era of more accurate astronomical observation. He then used more rigorous astronomical observation data to corroborate his theory in explaining various celestial phenomena, influencing astronomy for more than 1500 years. (cf https://baike.baidu.com/item/%E6%B5%91%E5%A4%A9%E8%AF%B4/717090?fromModule=lemma_inlink)

item.title: Paulo Maia and Carmo
item.description: Shen Deqian (1673-1769), whom Emperor Qianlong (r. 1736-95) called the "illustrious old scholar of Jiangnan", was a notable and influential high official, poet, essayist and master of thought with a career marked by successive surprises, such as the unusual fact that he only passed the difficult jinshi exam in 1739, after seventeen attempts and when he had already sixty-seven years. But soon after, he would assume important roles as deputy minister in the Ministry of Rites or Grand Tutor of the Royal Prince. A role that he had long performed for private individuals. The recognition that Qianlong showed him included not only the «imperial favor» that allowed him to stroll in the imperial gardens but also the rare privilege of having a temple erected in his name for which the emperor wrote with his brush the phrase: «Venerable dean of the world of poetry». Many decrees and memorials were written with the authority that possessed his brush, such as the one that states that in 1699, “Emperor Kangxi declared that from now on, Spring Dawn on the Su (Shi) Embankment will be the first name on the list of the Ten Views of the West Lake.” At the time of his death, the emperor himself composed an elegy for him. No one would deny the master's persistent and valiant exemplary career. But suddenly in 1778, he had already been dead for eight years, his honorary titles were taken away, his commemorative temple was abolished, his posthumous name was revoked, his tombstone was torn down, his body was even exhumed and quartered, among other punishments. And all because of the reference to a flower. During a period of literary inquisitions, a subtle reference to the perenniality of the previous dynasty was discovered in a work by Xu Shukui (1703-1763). That Shen Deqian, his biographer and fellow countryman from Jiangsu, where there was unstoppable resistance to Manchu power, praised and quoted in the verses: «Nothing but the color red can be the true one,/ Even if foreign varieties proclaim themselves «queen of flowers» (huawang, a popular name for peony). Both were severely condemned, even post-mortem. Wang Jiu (1745-98), a painter who Wang Yu included in his Oriental Estate Painting Manual at the end of the 18th century, Dongzhuang hualun, as one of the Four Little Wangs, recalled verses by Shen Deqian in the year before his conviction, in a small album (ink on paper, 13.8 x 20.5 cm, for sale at Christie's). On one of the pages there is just a willow tree, an undulating emblem of longing, and beneath it a fisherman in a boat evoking the desire to sail free over the danger of the waters. Which sometimes came only in an allusion to red peonies, and could even have a brutal punishment, but if one was skillful, as Shen Deqian wrote about Meng Haoran's poetry, «the language will be simple, but the flavor will remain.»

item.title: Today Macau
item.description: Tokyo on Tuesday urged Tehran to cease actions that threaten navigation in the Strait of Hormuz, as the debate intensifies over international intervention to protect maritime traffic in that fundamental route for energy trade. Japanese Foreign Minister Toshimitsu Motegi made the appeal on Tuesday night during a phone call with his Iranian counterpart, Abbas Araghchi, according to a statement from the head of Japanese diplomacy. “Minister Motegi strongly urged Iran to immediately cease attacks on civilian installations and infrastructure in the Gulf countries, as well as actions that threaten the safety of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz,” the statement revealed. The Japanese minister expressed deep concern about the continuation of the exchange of “retaliatory attacks” since the previous telephone call held on March 9, and with the extent of the damage, which even affected neighboring countries. Furthermore, Motegi expressed concern about the high number of ships linked to Japan that are currently stranded in the Persian Gulf and asked Iran to take the necessary measures to guarantee the safety of all ships in the Strait of Hormuz, “including those from Japan and other Asian countries”. For his part, Araghchi explained Iran's position and both ministers agreed to maintain close communication with a view to quickly easing tensions, according to the Japanese statement. Expectations of a potential deployment of Japanese military ships to the region could be one of the key themes at the summit this Thursday in Washington between Japanese Prime Minister, Sanae Takaichi, and the US President. Asked yesterday about the possibility of sending Japanese Self-Defense Forces personnel to the area, Takaichi reiterated that, for now, there are no plans to do so.

item.title: José Simões Morais
item.description: The Jesuit Matteo Ricci (1552-1610) initially interpreted Chinese rites as superstitious, but to understand them better he studied the doctrine of Confucius, on which Chinese society and its political system were based with the principles of Order, Peace and Harmony. The name Confucius was given by Ricci to Kong Fu Zi （孔夫子， 551-479 BC), master Kong (Kongzi), who was born in the Spring-Autumn Period (770-476 BC), two thousand years ago. He lived during the Eastern Zhou Dynasty, when hundreds of small kingdoms that had previously been federated began to be conquered and great instability arose due to continuous wars between the increasingly larger and stronger kingdoms. Without qualified rulers for this new space of strategic management, many enlightened masters appeared, including Kongzi, who offered their knowledge, placing them at the service of the king for governance. Kongzi reorganized ancient knowledge and, being a great educator, transmitted it to his disciples. He instilled respect for the Ancestors, as a way of life he proposed the improvement of ethics through righteousness, benevolence and virtues. As a philosophy, the path of practical wisdom for harmony in social life, education and politics, attaching great importance to Peace, Harmony and Order as a principle of good governance and organization of the State. After his death in 479 BC, the teachings, despite having been marginalized at the time, had Confucian followers, excellent and powerful administrators. In 213 B.C.E. Most of the writings were destroyed by fire, but reproduced from memory they were perpetuated in Chinese society from the Han dynasty onwards (206 BC-220). Kongzi advocated the concept of Harmony: an ordered Universe, in which human beings must seek to maintain harmonious relationships with Nature, with themselves and with others. He regulated human relationships, for which he found five fundamental ones: sovereign and subjects, parents and children, husband and wife, older brother and younger brothers and between friends. "If the duties between one another are observed, there is harmony; otherwise, disharmony reigns. The most important relationship is that of father and son and the central virtue that governs it is filial piety (孝, xiao). On this virtue and those that accompany it – obedience, respect and service – the structure of Chinese society was built." Between quotation marks is what I believe to be by Father Benjamim Videira Pires, but it could have another author and for this mistake I apologize and thank anyone who corrects and dispels this doubt. The article from here onwards is structured around this text, from which the citations come, as I believe the subject deserves enough importance to be published. The kow-tow in rites "Filial piety should not cease with the death of parents; the child must continue to serve them as if they were alive, and this duty extends to all ancestors of the family. Confucius, by extending filial piety to the dead, fostered this virtue in life", taking actions to be carried out as a show of respect for ancestors and the desire to achieve their perfections with the help of example, imitating them; – the best way to honor family. In memory of the Ancestors, rituals were carried out in the houses where wooden tablets were found inscribed with the names of the deceased male ancestors, father, grandfather and great-grandfather, as when the great-great-grandfather became the tablet the right to appear in the Family Temple, until after a few generations it entered that of the Ancestors. Expressions of reverence and respect were addressed to the tablets, in an environment with flowers, candles, incense, as the intention is “to arrange a certain place or term to which they can elevate their minds; and to which, as to a painting, they can direct the customary honors, as if they were present.” In this ceremony, "kau-táu (headbanging) was performed, in which a person kneels and bows deeply until their forehead touches the ground. It is the Chinese custom of respect: nine prostrations to the throne at imperial receptions; prostrations in the courts before magistrates and of children before their parents and in-laws. Thus, there is nothing religious about the prostrations at funerals before the coffin and it could not be said that it was worship of the dead." All this led Ricci to modify the original opinion: "what to the European seemed like superstition and worship, to the Chinese it was just a symbolic expression of filial piety. The fact that they used the same ceremonies for the living led us to believe that they were not religious acts when applied to the dead. Such was the case with the burning of incense (…). Educated people used to welcome guests with incense, which was part of the ceremonial etiquette, which gave grace and harmony to social relations, being a sign of respect. There was therefore no reason to give it a religious meaning when it was used at funerals, before tablets or in the rooms of Confucius.” After long discussions with Chinese friends, Ricci concluded that there was nothing idolatrous in the ancestral rites or perhaps superstitious and "submitted these opinions to Valignano, who discussed them with his consultants. The conclusion was that it was permitted to participate in the solemn rites in honor of the family's ancestors on the condition that paper money was not burned, but it was not permitted to take part in the solemn ceremonies in honor of Confucius with the sacrifice of a victim, because, as he said Furtado, “smell strongly of superstition” (Quoted by Dunne, Generation of Giants).” On days prescribed by the ritual, with the appearance of religious sacrifices, an animal (immolated on site), wine, silk… was offered to the ancestors, following a family banquet. According to the Jesuits, “The idea of ​​spirits taking nourishment from the food offered should be repudiated; and of not making prayers or requests to the dead”. Other ceremonies the Jesuits permitted and even observed. “On the occasion of someone's death, the friends, who came to offer their condolences, would wear, in mourning, a white silk robe; upon entering the room, they would perform the kau-t'au four times in front of the deceased; the deceased's children would then repeat the same gesture; the visitor, approaching closer to the coffin, would perform the same ceremony again. The rites in honor of Confucius were only practiced by literate people and did not differ in essence from ancestral ceremonies,” and after “passing the exams, prostrations were made before Confucius’ tablet in a room dedicated to him. The same rites, prostrations and burning of incense were observed by officials and scholars on the days of the new moon and full moon.” Confucianism is based on the teachings of Kong Fuzi, a reformer who, using the rites of the Zhou, reorganized the order of society and structured human thought in China through the concepts of virtue, justice, tolerance and generosity. For practical wisdom, the importance of Astronomy and the preparation of the Calendar, as any inaccuracy disturbs the balance between Heaven and Earth. 完

item.description: Shen Deqian (1673-1769), whom Emperor Qianlong (r. 1736-95) called the "illustrious old scholar of Jiangnan", was a notable and influential high official, poet, essayist and master of thought with a career marked by successive surprises, such as the unusual fact that he only passed the difficult jinshi exam in 1739, after seventeen attempts and when he had already sixty-seven years. item.title: Culture | There is a lack of public support to build a book market
item.description: Small market, little sales outside the territory and lack of support: book publishing in Macau faces problems in solidifying itself and competing with what is done abroad. Representative Lam Fat Iam recently defended policies to support the construction of a “Reading City”. Pin-to Livros & Música and the publisher Mandarina point out gaps that impede the dynamism of the sector It was in February, in an intervention before the agenda, that deputy Lam Fat Iam launched the challenge in the Legislative Assembly (AL): “support the development of local physical bookstores and build a ‘Reading City'”. In the text, read in the hemicycle, the deputy appointed by the Government argued that Macau, as the “World Center for Tourism and Leisure” is a region where bookstores “are not mere places to sell books, but also essential cultural spaces in communities, playing a crucial role in promoting the diversification of the tourism sector and sustainable development of the city”. Lam Fat Iam knows what he's talking about. Born in July 1975, he has a PhD in History and presides over the Macau Cultural Education Publications Association, in addition to being director of the Faculty of Human and Social Sciences at the Macau Polytechnic University. In the same intervention, the person responsible suggested that “specific and precise support policies” should be created, namely the creation of “support programs for the exploration and cultural activities of bookstores”, giving “priority to bookstores that promote Macau culture and regularly hold conferences and reading activities”. A system must also be created that encourages “the circulation and publication of local books”, with “priority financing for the planning, editing and publication of books with Macau characteristics”. HM questioned bookstores and publishers in order to understand the gaps in the Macau book market. Anson Ng, from Pin-to Livros & Música, draws attention to the fact that “the market base, in itself, is small, with the book-reading population registering a decrease”. Conversely, “operational costs remain high and there is competition from external online bookstore platforms”. Anson Ng further added that “Macau’s local publishers and local bookstores have not yet managed to form a self-sufficient, healthy and sustainable ecosystem, and that is precisely where supporting policies should focus their efforts”. Regarding the position of deputy Lam Fat Iam, the head of Pin-to Livros & Música says that his ideas follow “an acceptable direction”, however, “an in-depth discussion” is necessary regarding “details of implementation” of the ideas expressed in the interpellation. “Before establishing concrete support policies, we hope that relevant government departments can carefully study the experiences of other regions”, in addition to maintaining “extensive communication with the current industry in Macau”. For Anson Ng, we must also try to “truly understand the needs of the sector”, as “only in this way will it be possible to develop transparent, fair and practical support policies”. Contact difficulties Catarina Mesquita, founder of Mandarina, which publishes children's books, does not have a physical store, but sells her books in spaces such as Livraria Portuguesa. In response to HM, Catarina Mesquita suggests “that the Government should, together with schools, be able to place orders with bookstores and directly with publishers”, speaking of the specific case of “Na Rua”, one of the Mandarina editions that she tried to place directly in schools. "The book 'Na Rua' talks about the history of Macau and I have tried, many times, for this book to be introduced in schools, not only because it has a history of the place where these children live, but also because it has many aspects of the history of Macau, and very specific characteristics [of the territory], which is talked about so much. And I have tried several times to contact the education services [Directorate of Education and Youth Development Services], I was unable to get that contact and it is very difficult. I have had people from the Government in presentation sessions about the Mandarin, but I didn’t get any feedback”, he laments. Catarina Mesquita therefore argues that the Government should “together with schools, place orders with bookstores and directly with publishers”. "You end up falling into a hole that you don't really know what it is. This effort should be more valued, not in the sense of being praised, but in the sense of taking Macau's works and taking them into classrooms, which is the most efficient and effective way of getting them to children, in the case of children's literature. I think it is not something that is very difficult to do", he assumed. The subsidy issue It is true that the Executive has presented some measures to promote reading, and Lam Fat Iam noted this in his plenary intervention. “In recent years, the Macao SAR Government and society have already achieved results in promoting reading and building a ‘Reading City'”, he described, particularly on the part of the Cultural Institute, with the creation of events such as the “Reading Festival”, “Library Week”, “Month of Joint Reading throughout the City of Macau” and the “Reading Program for Babies and Children”. There is, in his view, a “solid basis for a social environment favorable to reading”, but “physical bookstores, as essential spaces for reading, face survival difficulties”, highlighted the deputy. Lam Fat Iam also highlighted “the limited size of the market, high rental costs and a fragile chain of production and sales of local publications created an environment with ‘difficulty in publishing, buying and selling books'”. Something that, in the deputy's opinion, “makes it difficult for many valuable Macau books to reach the hands of residents and visitors”. Catarina Mesquita also draws attention to the fact that Macau has many published books and is “a very rich territory” in terms of its history and culture, but that more needs to be done beyond the simple subsidy to publish a book. In the case of Mandarina, the first five books were made “without any subsidies or support”. "Perhaps due to a personality trait, I didn't want Mandarina, in the beginning, to be the result of support or subsidies. I wanted what I achieved to be done by me, and that made me work much harder", he assures. Catarina Mesquita therefore feels that the financial support, or this initial subsidy, “is enough to produce a book, and that’s it”, when, in fact, “this support is more important later in terms of distribution, ordering, people being able to have the book in their hands”. And he argues that more should be done to support the distribution of works. “There is no support, or bridge” in this sense, he considers. "There is so much talk about the platform with Portuguese-speaking countries, but there is no support for the distribution and transport of books to regions other than Macau, and this is fundamental. I have come across this problem, because at the moment Mandarina is starting its first steps in Portugal and I have no way of getting the books to the country without having any type of support." In the responsible's view, the connection between Portugal and the territory could be explored more effectively. "In terms of laws and taxes we pay on imports, a partnership that would make us benefit from it made sense. Therefore, I think there is a hole here that needs to be resolved quickly." The four measures Lam Fat Iam also left, in his intervention, suggestions to improve the distribution system for editorial works, defending the construction of a “virtuous system for the circulation and publication of local books”, with “priority financing for the planning, editing and publication of books with Macau characteristics”. Regarding “public services, public libraries, community libraries and primary and secondary schools financed by the Government, must place orders for books in local bookstores”. Furthermore, in connection with urban renewal projects, the deputy believes that the “Bookstore +” model can be developed, defining “guidelines” and reserving “cultural spaces to encourage the integration of bookstores with cafes, art galleries, cultural and creative stores and even revitalization projects for historic buildings”. For the deputy, bookstores must be “multifunctional cultural landmarks that integrate reading, leisure, exhibitions and cultural exchange”, and there must also be support for participation in local and regional book fairs. HM contacted other bookstores in Macau, but at the time of writing this edition had received no further responses.

item.title: Iran | War breaks out in the Middle East with attacks that killed Khamenei
item.description:
The United States and Israel launched a series of attacks against Iran. One of the fatal victims was Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, as well as several Iranian military leaders. The Shah's heir, Reza Pahlavi, believes in a bright future, at a time when Tehran's counterattacks are spreading across the Middle East
Israel and the United States of America (USA) joined together in a joint operation to overthrow the Iranian regime, at a time when, in the country, there were already some demonstrations against the authorities. On Saturday, a series of bombings were launched against Iran, with the declared aim of forcing a change of regime. The country responded with attacks on Israel and US military bases, and the death of Iran's supreme religious leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who had been in power for 36 years, was also confirmed. According to Lusa, the announcement was made on Iranian state television at 5am local time, with a reporter crying over what had happened. Khameinei was 86 years old and, for Trump, his death now offers the Iranian population a “greater chance” of “recovering” the country. The Iranian regime declared 40 days of mourning for the death of the religious leader. In addition to Khamenei, the chief of staff of the Iranian Armed Forces, Abdolrahim Mousavi, was also killed, along with other high-ranking generals during the US and Israeli attacks against the country, Iranian state television reported yesterday. Meanwhile, China expressed “great concern” about the situation in the Middle East and called for respect for Iran's sovereignty and territorial integrity. “Iran's sovereignty, security and territorial integrity must be respected,” said the Chinese Ministry of Foreign Affairs in a statement cited by the North American news agency The Associated Press (AP). Beijing called for “an immediate cessation of military actions and that there is no further escalation of a tense situation”. He also called for the “resumption of dialogue and negotiation” and efforts to “maintain peace and stability in the Middle East”, according to the statement also cited by the Spanish news agency Europa Press (EP). China has recommended the departure of its citizens from Iran, advising its citizens not to travel to the region, due to the “marked increase in security risks”, as the country faces repeated threats of attacks from the US. “Chinese citizens currently in Iran should reinforce their security precautions and leave the country as soon as possible,” the Consular Affairs Department of the Chinese Ministry of Foreign Affairs said in a statement. A nuclear issue North American President Donald Trump has threatened to attack Iran due to the authorities' repression of protests against the regime and alleging that Tehran is developing nuclear weapons, despite having attacked sites on Iranian soil in June precisely with the aim of stopping the alleged nuclear program. At the end of the campaign, Trump claimed that Iran's nuclear program had been completely obliterated. On Tuesday - and despite talks in Geneva about an agreement regarding Iran's nuclear program - Trump once again said, during his State of the Nation address in Congress, that he will not allow Iran to have nuclear weapons. In 2015, Iran signed an agreement to limit its nuclear development – ​​in exchange for the relief of economic sanctions on the country – with the five permanent members of the UN Security Council — the United States, Russia, China, France and the United Kingdom, as well as with Germany and the European Union. However, the agreement lost effect after Trump himself unilaterally withdrew the United States from the treaty in 2018, during his first term (2017-2021). China's recommendations join those of several other countries that have raised their security alerts, such as Sweden, Serbia, Germany, India, South Korea, Brazil, Australia, the United Kingdom or Canada. Tension in the Middle East Ali Khamenei's death puts the future of the Islamic Republic in doubt and increases the risk of regional instability. “Khamenei, one of the most evil people in history, is dead,” Trump wrote in a social media post, warning of the continuation of “heavy and precise bombings” throughout the week and even beyond. Saturday's attack opened a new chapter in the US intervention in Iran, bringing with it the potential for retaliatory violence and a broader war. The truth is that there are already pockets of tension thanks to the Iranian response, with attacks on around ten countries, including the United Arab Emirates. In Dubai, a strong explosion occurred in a luxury hotel area, causing four injuries, while Dubai International Airport was also the target of an attack, leading to the cancellation of all flights. Another source of tension occurred in Karachi, Pakistan, resulting in the death of six people and 20 injuries during a protest near the fortified area of ​​the United States Consulate. According to local police spokesman Rehan Ali, to CNN, “hundreds of people suddenly appeared in the vicinity” of the diplomatic mission, leading to a rapid mobilization of security forces. The screams captured on the recordings suggest that the protest was motivated by attacks involving the United States and Israel against neighboring Iran. A country without a leader Khamenei's death in the Trump Administration's second attack on Iran in eight months creates a leadership vacuum, given the absence of a known ayatollah successor, and also because the 86-year-old supreme leader had the final say on all major policies during decades in power. Khamenei led Iran's clerical establishment and its paramilitary Revolutionary Guard, the two main centers of power in the ruling theocracy. Ali Larijani, secretary of Iran's National Security Council, said on Saturday that Israel and the United States "will regret their actions." “The brave soldiers and the great nation of Iran will teach an unforgettable lesson to the infernal international oppressors,” Larijani posted on the X social network. The joint operation by the United States and Israel, which according to authorities was planned for months, took place this Saturday, during the Muslim holy month of Ramadan and at the beginning of the Iranian work week, and followed negotiations and warnings from Trump, who last year trumpeted his government's success in crippling the country's nuclear program. About 12 hours after the attacks began, the U.S. military reported that there were no American casualties and that damage to U.S. bases was minimal despite “hundreds of Iranian missile and drone strikes.” According to US forces, targets in Iran included Revolutionary Guard command facilities, air defense systems, missile and drone launch sites and military airfields. Regarding Khamenei's death, Trump declared that the ayatollah “was unable to escape highly sophisticated intelligence and tracking systems”. “Working closely with Israel, there was nothing he, or the other leaders who were killed with him, could do,” said the American President. “This is the greatest opportunity for the Iranian people to take back their country,” he said. An Iranian diplomat told the United Nations Security Council that hundreds of civilians were killed and injured in the attacks. Iran retaliated by firing missiles and drones at Israel and US military bases in the region, and fighting continued into the night. US Democrats criticized the fact that Trump took measures without authorization from Congress, but White House spokeswoman Karoline Leavitt said that the Administration informed several Republican and Democratic leaders in Congress in advance. What does the Shah's son say At a time when regime change appears to be imminent in Iran, but with no clear understanding of how it will happen, one of the voices that has defended US and Israeli action is Reza Pahlavi, son of the former Shah. Having lived in the USA for decades, since the Iranian revolution of 1979, Pahlavi is now arguing that it is time to take to the streets. According to Lusa, the heir stated, in an editorial published in The Washington Post, that he intends to take on a role in the transition process in Iran. "Many Iranians, often despite bullets, asked me to lead this transition. I am impressed by your courage and responded to your call", he wrote. "Our path forward will be transparent: a new Constitution written and ratified by referendum, followed by free elections under international supervision. When Iranians vote, the transitional government will be dissolved." For the past 47 years, Pahlavi has been a critical voice of the Iranian regime, now siding with Trump and Israel. Domestically and regionally, Pahlavi is widely regarded as a puppet of Washington and Tel Aviv. In the article, he praises the entire trajectory that Trump has taken towards Iran, saying that the country's “rebirth” “will mark the beginning of an era of peace and prosperity”. Pahlavi also highlighted that “Iran is not Iraq”. "We will not repeat the mistakes that followed this [Iraq] conflict. There will be no dissolution of institutions, no power vacuum, no chaos", he said, recalling that since last year he has been defending the so-called "Iran Prosperity Project".
