Through photography, the competition invites participants to reveal how these human dimensions give character and vitality to architecture", describes CURB in a statement. This time, the competition will be divided into two categories, one open to all residents and the other aimed at student work. The photographs will be evaluated by a panel led by Ines Leong, joined by jurors Francisco Ricarte, Nuno Soares and Carlos Sena Caires. A mirror of the city The winners of the competition receive cash prizes and certificates from the organization attesting to the recognition. In the open group, the first prize winner will receive 3,000 patacas, the second 2,000 patacas and the third 1,000 patacas. In the student category, cash prizes are half the value. The competition is open to holders of a valid resident identity card (permanent or non-permanent), holders of a blue-card or Macau student card. In the student category, it is mandatory to enroll in higher or secondary education establishments in Macau, or abroad, and be under 30 years of age. Once the requirements have been met, simply upload the photographs to the CURB portal. Since its debut edition in 2022, the Macau Architectural Photography Competition has entered the city's cultural agenda, "driving enthusiastic participation from photographers of all levels, from professionals, amateurs, students to the general public. Over this period, the competition attracted 688 participants and received 1,611 applications, presenting exceptional results and growing interest", indicates the organization. As usual, after the winners are announced, the works distinguished by the jury will be exposed to the public.

item.title: FRC hosts lecture on ancestral Christian practices from a feminine perspective
item.description: The Rui Cunha Foundation (FRC) presents today, starting at 6:30 pm, the lecture “Itinerarium Egeriae and frescoes from Catacombs: History and Iconography in Feminine Writing and Funerary Images”, given by associate professor at the State University of Ceará (UECE) Sílvia Siqueira. The lecture, moderated by professor Adérito Fernandes-Marcos, from the University of São José, will have as its starting point the itinerary of Egéria's pilgrimage through a “comparative analysis between the written text, especially the biblical passages and some images present in catacombs and funeral monuments of women, created between the 3rd century and the 4th century AD”, describes the event's proposal. Egeria is credited with authoring a book that portrays the pilgrimage through the Pax Romana between what is now northern Italy and Syria, with several wanderings through Jerusalem and present-day Istanbul. Sílvia Siqueira considers that female figures, in early Christian art, played significant roles in the transmission of faith. "Egeria, a devout Christian pilgrim, left a record of her pilgrimage trip to the Holy Land. It is a rare and precious document, as it is one of the few records written in antiquity by a woman. She describes not only the trip, but also narrates in detail the experience of the pilgrimage and the Christian liturgical practice in Jerusalem", describe the academic. The lecture and the city Taking the author's writings from the times of Ancient Rome, between three and four centuries after Christ, Sílvia Siqueira considers that although the documents were written by a fervent pilgrim, there is not much information about the practical lives of devout Christians. As such, the academic proposes a comparative analysis between the account of Egéria's experience with images of women found on the walls of Christian catacombs, countless paintings and mosaics that testify to the figurative culture of the time that produced it. The session, which has free entry, is part of the Doctoral Lectures in the City series, coordinated by professor Adérito Fernandes-Marcos, Director of the Doctoral School of the University of São José, who will assume the role of moderator.

item.title: Excursion | Forced purchases investigated in Macau, HK and Zhuhai
item.description: Authorities in Shenzhen are investigating a case of forced purchases that allegedly occurred on a tour of Macau, Zhuhai and Hong Kong. The situation was revealed by the Chinese newspaper Jimu News, which infiltrated the low-cost tour, which will have cost tourists 380 yuan for a five-day trip to the three cities, between the 8th and 12th of March. The report revealed forced purchasing practices and constant pressure to purchase products from selected stores on the tour organized by the Yantian (Shenzhen) district branch of Hengsheng International Travel Agency. The low-cost trip attracted mostly elderly people who ended up spending most of their time in outlet stores, where store security guards prevented them from leaving until they had permission from the tour guide. The newspaper reports that elderly people ended up being coerced into buying medicines worth thousands of yuan, including three “middle-aged” people who reportedly spent around 10,000 yuan in a single day. When expenses fell short of expectations, the guide reacted with anger and shouting, threatening to continue visiting stores until the purchases satisfied the merchants involved in the scheme. The guide even threatened to cut off meals to two excursionists who, after spending more than 1,000 yuan each, asked that the focus of the tour be more on points of tourist interest instead of shops. The culture and tourism department of Yantian district was visited by authorities who initiated mediation procedures between injured parties and the travel agency.

item.title: Jardim Nova Taipa | Fire sends man to hospital
item.description: A man was found on the floor where the fire broke out with second-degree burns to his face, hands and feet. He was transported to the hospital in “stable” condition. The flames started in an apartment that was being renovated A 65-year-old man had to be hospitalized yesterday after suffering burns due to a fire in Urbanização Jardim Nova Taipa. According to information from the Fire Department (CB), the flames broke out yesterday morning, around 9 am, in an apartment that was being renovated, and the injured person was one of the workers on site. When rescue teams reached the 24th floor, where the fire originated, they found the man on the floor, with second-degree burns on his face, hands and feet. The worker was removed from the building by rescue teams and transported to the Centro Hospitalar Conde de São Januário, where authorities indicated that he is in a stable condition and far from danger. It was also reported that several residents received treatment in ambulances at the scene. In the apartment where the fire started, and which was being renovated, firefighters came across open flames that were extinguished using water hoses. Furthermore, to speed up the removal of smoke from inside the apartment, rescue teams used extraction fans. According to the newspaper Ou Mun, the rescued man recognized that before the explosion there had been a gas leak. However, the space was ventilated and work continued. The explosion occurred when a mower created a spark that led to the explosion. Run for life According to the Chinese channel Radio Macau, when residents of the building noticed the flames they began to abandon their apartments, which led to 50 people leaving on their own. Speaking to the newspaper Ou Mun, some residents who were outside the building, while firefighters were fighting the fire, said they heard a large explosion and felt the building shaking. After that moment, they saw flames coming from inside the affected apartment. Also a resident with the surname Ho, who lives close to the affected building, stated that he had returned home when the explosion was heard. He went out into the street to see what was going on and noticed that there were pieces of glass on the sidewalk. Due to the fight against the flames, several sections of Rua de Bragança and Rua de Foshan were temporarily closed to traffic. In reaction to the fire, the Institute of Social Action (IAS) stated that it was monitoring the situation and providing accommodation for affected residents. “The Social Action Institute is deeply concerned about the fire that occurred at Urbanização Jardim Nova Taipa, in Taipa”, it was announced. “As soon as the IAS became aware of the incident, it coordinated with social service agencies in Taipa to send teams to the scene to provide support and comfort to the affected residents,” it added. According to yesterday morning's statement, there had been no request for temporary accommodation.

item.title: Flu | Number of children who went to hospital almost doubles
item.description: The Health Services launched an alert for the increase in flu cases, with the rate of positive tests for the influenza virus reaching almost 18 percent, exceeding the threshold that requires an alert to be issued. Authorities call for vaccination, especially pregnant women, children and the elderly The Health Services issued an alert on Tuesday regarding the increase in flu cases in Macau. “Laboratory results show that the positivity rate in influenza virus tests gradually increased from 5 percent in the week of February 22 to 28 to 17.8 percent last week (March 8 to 14), exceeding the alert level of 15.2 percent,” the authorities specify. More than 70 percent of these cases involved the influenza B virus, which represents an evolution compared to previous periods of high influenza prevalence. The two previous peak phases of the influenza virus in Macau were between mid-November 2025 and early January 2026, with the H3 subtype of influenza A virus being the predominant strain in circulation. According to data from the emergency services of the three hospitals in the region, there was a significant increase in consultations for children due to flu-like symptoms. The number of consultations gradually increased from the end of February, rising from 177 per week between 22 and 28 February, to 342 per week last week (8 to 14 March). The Health Services emphasize that the volume of adult consultations due to similar symptoms has remained “relatively stable”. Better to prevent With the virus returning to a higher level of activity, the Health Services appealed to the population to take precautions, especially pregnant women, young children, the elderly and people with chronic illnesses who have not yet been vaccinated against the flu. The authorities reinforced that these demographic groups “are more likely to suffer serious complications or even die, so they should be vaccinated as quickly as possible”. Authorities revealed on Tuesday that five collective cases of flu affected schools in the territory, with infections exceeding fifty. Regarding these types of outbreaks in kindergartens and schools, the most recent cases are mainly caused by the influenza B virus. “Cases gradually increased from mid-February, going from zero in the first three weeks to 10 last week, with nine cases registered in the first three days of this week”, indicated the Health Services, noting that there have been no serious cases of flu since January.

item.title: Kong Chi case | Sentence increased to 24 years in prison
item.description: The Court of Second Instance (TSI) yesterday concluded the second trial of Kong Chi, former Deputy Prosecutor of the Macao SAR, and increased his sentence from 21 years in prison to 24 years. The panel of judges found Kong guilty of several crimes of corruption, malfeasance and abuse of power. In relation to the other defendants, lawyer Ho Kam Meng was sentenced to 15 years in prison and businesswoman Choi Sao Ieng was sentenced to 16 years and 9 months in prison. Lawyer and fourth defendant Kuan Hoi Lon, who was never present during the trial, was acquitted of all charges. According to the newspaper Ou Mun, during the reading of the sentence, judge Choi Mou Pan considered that it had been proven that Kong Chi used his position in the Public Prosecutor's Office to dismiss or request the acquittal of defendants in several cases, receiving various benefits in return. This activity was developed together with lawyer Ho Kam Meng and businesswoman Choi Sao Ieng. Choi Mou Pan also pointed out that when transferring sovereignty, Kong Chi swore an oath to comply with legal principles, integrity and safeguard the interests of the Macao SAR. However, for the judges it was proven that Kong abused his powers for personal gain, violating the laws. The court also highlighted that the various crimes proven were only possible due to the attitude of the former Deputy Prosecutor and that without him they would not have occurred. Choi Mou Pan also stated that Kong Chi's conduct “had a profound impact on Macau”. List of accusations The former Deputy Prosecutor of the Macao SAR was accused of 79 crimes, including 56 crimes of malfeasance, all co-authored with Ho Kam Meng, and another 23 crimes of passive corruption for an illicit act. Ho Kam was accused of creating/being part of a secret association. This association involves Kong Chi and Choi Sai Ieng, who were convicted of this crime in the first trial. The lawyer was also accused of another 68 crimes, including 12 crimes of passive corruption for an illicit act and 56 crimes of malfeasance co-authored with Kong Chi. In the case of lawyer Ho Kam Meng, the court found that he was a member of and provided support to the association or secret society, to which Kong Chi and Choi Sao Ieng also belonged, a crime for which they had already been convicted in the first trial. As for Kuan Hoi Lon, the lawyer was found innocent, as it was considered that it had not been proven that she had acted beyond her legal powers.

item.title: Financial crisis at FC Porto de Macau compromises continuity
item.description: The subsidiary of Futebol Clube do Porto in Macau is going through a financial and operational crisis that could jeopardize its continuity, according to a letter sent to members by management. In the letter, the director of Casa do Futebol Clube do Porto de Macau – China, Diana Massada, pointed out that the club is facing “serious financial and operational difficulties”, which recently culminated in an eviction process from the headquarters and the existence of several unresolved debts. Massada recalls that, over eight years, the current management dedicated time and personal resources that helped to overcome specific difficulties and allow the club to continue to exist, but admits that “the situation has become particularly difficult” in the last year and a half. Among the factors highlighted are the lack of regular payment of dues – only around ten out of more than 100 members have their dues up to date, the absence of sponsors, structural problems in the leased headquarters and difficulties in operating the entity's canteen, which should be one of the main sources of revenue. The president also highlights that the active participation of members has been limited, which has limited the ability to develop projects and overcome demanding moments. Multiple appeals In the letter, the management appeals for the support of members to regularize quotas, identify sponsors and collaborate on projects such as the Football School or youth volleyball. One of the immediate needs mentioned is to remove and sell assets from the old headquarters to settle small debts. “The club is going through a difficult time, but continues to represent many years of dedication, coexistence, sports practice and community spirit”, wrote Diana Massada, appealing to the “collective will” of the members to guarantee the continuity of the House of FC Porto de Macau. FC Porto de Macau was founded in 2006 and quickly ascended to the I Division of football in the territory, where it came second. The last time the club was present in Macau's first division was in 2012, with financial problems pushing the team down to the last division. FC Porto de Macau's senior eleven football team fell from the third to the fourth division last season, two seasons after winning promotion, following 10 defeats, one draw and zero wins.

item.title: Macao Water | Expected 3% increase in consumption
item.description: Last year was marked by a reduction in water consumption, contrary to expectations. The movement of residents outside the territory during holidays and weekends was indicated as the cause of the contraction The executive director of the Macau Water Supply Society (Macao Water), Nacky Kuan Sio Peng, predicts an increase in water consumption in Macau throughout this year of around 3 percent. The scenario was outlined by the company manager on the sidelines of a Lunar New Year Lunch with media outlets. The director justified the estimate with the opening of more hotels and housing. “Although the construction works have been completed [which leads to a reduction in consumption], we expect an annual increase of three percent in water consumption, because the inhabitants of Zone A of the New Urban Landfills and lot P in Areia Preta will move into housing, and also because some hotel projects on the islands will be inaugurated and will come into operation”, said Nacky Kuan. Regarding the use of recycled water, which began to be supplied on March 1st to public housing in Seac Pai Van and the University of Macau, Nacky Kuan gave a positive assessment of the services and indicated that currently the daily average supply of this type of water is 1.5 thousand cubic meters. Recycled water is a type of non-potable water, which receives minimal treatment to be used for flushing toilets and watering green spaces. Lower consumption In relation to the balance of water consumption last year, the company recognized that there was a reduction of 0.7 percent. “Although many events and concerts were held, and the number of tourists registered an ideal increase, which resulted in an increase in water supply on the islands of 3 percent, the number was affected by the drop in water consumption by residents and the end of several constructions”, explained the person in charge. The reduction in water consumption goes against the company's expectations, which in 2025 predicted a 3 percent increase in consumption, which ended up not happening. Nacky Kuan considered that consumption by residents abroad was the reason that resulted in the drop in water consumption, particularly during holidays and weekends. The official also pointed out that water consumption by small and medium-sized companies has not yet recovered to pre-pandemic levels. In terms of inter-regional cooperation, last year, Macao Water joined the Guangdong-Hong Kong-Macao Greater Bay Area Water Conservation Sector Alliance, with the aim of jointly promoting innovation in the field of water conservation. Furthermore, the company also participated, for the first time, as a member of the organizing committee in a symposium promoted by the International Water Association. The company also took stock of several works completed last year, such as, for example, the expansion of the Seac Pai Van Dam, as well as the installation of the water supply network and shared underground pipelines in Zone A. Also at the beginning of this year, the second phase of the work to expand the Pinggang Pumping Station, in Zhuhai, came into operation.

item.title: Firefighters | Almost 6 thousand inspections of residential buildings
item.description: In 2025, the Fire Department carried out almost 6,000 fire safety inspections of local buildings. The number was revealed through the response to a question from deputy Ho Ion Sang “regarding the issue of misuse of common areas for individual purposes by condominiums. Throughout 2025, the Fire Department (CB) carried out a total of 11,760 fire safety inspections and checks, including 5,957 inspections in residential buildings”, it was communicated, in a response signed by the president of the Housing Institute (IH), Iam Lei Leng. “In addition to carrying out inspections in accordance with previously defined plans, the CB also responds to requests from managers, building managers or competent departments, or when it receives complaints about impacts on fire safety, sending personnel to the site to carry out verification and appropriate treatment”, it was added. In the response, it was not stated how many requests for correction of the detected situations were made or whether fines or other sanctions were applied. In relation to the environment in community neighborhoods, and the abandonment of garbage or dripping water from the air conditioning, according to the Institute of Municipal Affairs, “more than 14 thousand accusations” were registered for infractions. “In 2025, the IAM applied more than 14 thousand charges in relation to the situations mentioned”, it was indicated.

item.title: ZELENSKY VS ZELENSKY
item.description:
10/27/22 A Ukrainian pensioner, with the same name as the president, blames Zelensky for the prolongation of the conflict that destroyed his village. Zelensky, the villager, who never left his land, is now mortified because his favorite gravedigger died in the bomber. This Zelensky, a villager, represents half the world that recriminates the other Zelensky, the villain, for not wanting to negotiate with Putin. Negotiate what? The invader enters our house, covers his wife and daughter (cf. recent UN report on the rapes in Ukraine, in this “special operation”), an act that forced us to watch, and then we say to him: hi there, we are for peace, but we have to negotiate: you still haven't said what name you want to give the children! A peace that is not dignified is nothing more than a cessation of rights. As serious as the Russophobia that Putin inflicts on his people.
10/28/22 At a lunch with several guests, I heard a friend, who declared herself a spiritualist, explaining the particularities of her belief, in a calm and balanced way, I would even say, without a hint of “irrationalism”. He spoke at length and serenely, devoid of any fanatical proselytism. “Here is someone whose belief has brought thoughtfulness!” I thought, a rare alliance. However, at a certain moment a sentence comes out that horrifies me. It says, “there is no chance, nothing happens by chance!”, and I glimpse the voracious maws of holism. Because holism has a pathological aspect, like all good things, a shadow side. Two examples: in Hinduism there is less sensitivity to existential dramas, if that child suffers in this life this is just an expression of rigorous karmic symmetry, an effect of the acts he committed in his previous life. In Africa, it is not accepted that death could have been accidental, and many women are accused by the family of the deceased of having caused the death of their husband, in an act of manifest dementia, and, in the blatant majority of cases, a huge injustice for the wife — madness that spreads and infiltrates the social fabric. Buddhism introduced Compassion and Christianity introduced Caritas, trying to break with this logic, but in many other beliefs and religions the cage of causalities prevails over sensitivity to experience, the need to respond to the now. It is a tremendous logic that transforms the world into a Platonic stage, where we are nothing more than the shadows of something — an inextricable law -, in a determinism that calcifies all singularities, but that strangely fascinates many. The conquest of modernity was anchored in the conquest of chance and randomness, removing the incidences of a life from this fierce determinism that enclosed the world in a web. When the Renaissance freed the body from the influence of the stars, at that moment sexuality was relieved of guilt and individualism and its volition were able to emerge. There is an unequivocal moral implication in the phrase “there is no such thing as chance, nothing happens by chance!”, which should function as a key to self-knowledge and self-judgment, however, this phrase is usually used to seek a reason for things outside of us, in others. It's an alibi. Today there is a holistic temptation in the world, just as there was a satanic temptation in the Middle Ages, without much reflection on its consequences. Because it's not enough to want to connect everything in a resonant chain of affection. Even for the most unexpected: affection itself can kill. I realize, thanks to that cultured, balanced, intelligent woman, that “the fight” took place at the beginning of this century. One of the great mistakes of the 20th century was the false dichotomy between “rationalism and irrationalism”, a debate that extended to all fields, including art. First, rationality (the positive side of reason) was confused with rationalism (the pathological aspect of reason), and articulated in this lack of discernment, irrationalism was confused with freedom. Almost all 20th century art, with Surrealism at its head, made this mistake. The fallacy still exists despite the emergence of “irrationalism” and the consequent relativist currents that followed it having been consolidated everywhere. Essentially, much of what happened in the 20th century revolved around this clash between two paradigms: Rationalism versus Irationalism. Now, I see, we must save indeterminacy, chance, randomness from the terrible temptation of the cage of causalities. Holism, in its pathological form, may be reductionism that sets itself up as a new “eschatological narrative”. Is it “by chance” that holism emerges at a time when neo-liberalism and its cynical contempt for the human person is besieging everything, all freedoms, all acquired rights? Holistic awareness has brought a positive impact to greater ecological awareness, but followed with scholastic rigor, it stifles reality under the blanket of a determinism that is much more than uncomfortable: all great authoritarian ideologies are holistic. My friend will know how to combine her holistic and transpersonal belief with the freedom and responsibility that each moment asks of us — but how many will make the distinction, under a Law that explains and encompasses everything? How many will abandon themselves to what is? The awareness of the indeterminate brings tragic awareness, as the Greeks knew, but it also brings agency and the courage of decision. And we cannot give up on this. Furthermore, as Drummond shows in the poem “Quadrilha”, by Carlos Drummond de Andrade: «João loved Teresa who loved Raimundo/ who loved Maria who loved Joaquim who loved Lili/ who didn't love anyone./ João went to the United States, Teresa to the convent,/ Raimundo died of a disaster, Maria stayed with her aunt,/ Joaquim committed suicide and Lili married J. Pinto Fernandes/ who had not entered the story.» Real always runs outside the track, completely outside our concepts. That's what matters to us — a J. Pinto Fernandes emerges at the exact moment when we thought we had everything under control.

item.title: From Nobel and others
item.description: 9/10/22 When reading many people's reactions when this year's Nobel Prize for Literature was announced, I couldn't help but remember this comment from Jean-Pierre Siméon, in relation to another Nobel Prize: «An editor of a large national daily newspaper wrote five lines of indignation at the fact that the Nobel Prize for Literature had been awarded to Thomas Traströmer, a complete stranger. But unknown to whom? How did it not occur to this literary columnist, patented and paid to do his job, that his apparent ignorance was a matter of professional misconduct? Tranströmer had been translated into many languages, it was available in French.» There is always the chorus of those who wait for the Prize to be announced to complain about not having been invited to be on the jury, there is a subgroup of angry people who profess “I have never read it and I won’t even bother to read it”; It is a less than humble fauna, the one that inhabits social networks. I don't always know all the winners – I didn't know about the existence of Louise Glück, who I think is a great poet, or Olga Tokarczuk, for example – but this only brings me the comfort of knowing that the world is not a soap opera where everyone knows everyone else and the joy of recognizing that the yardstick of the world is not measured with my short measure and that, on the contrary, it gives me a new opportunity to face the unknown and learn from it. And perhaps the most stupid reaction was that of the intelligent man who wrote, But are there still people who read French?; simply confirming that today there is a new type of ignorance that is being raised with abandon: that of those who only read English. I would also prefer that, in French, Pascal Quignard, Linda Lê or even Sollers were awarded, or, among the new ones, Mathias Énard, but that does not detract from the dignity of Annie Ernaux's work (and it would have been enough to have written “Les Années”). There are books of hers that I like less, like the vulgar “Uma Paixão Simples”, but that happens with almost all authors, they have pivotal books and others that are like breaks in their itinerary. I recommend reading a beautiful book of interviews with her, “L’écriture comme un couteau”. 11/10/22 Yesterday an article appeared in the morning newspaper “Notícias”, in Maputo, reporting that the Pen Club Narrative Prize had been awarded to my wife, Teresa Noronha, for her soap opera “Tornado”, which had already won the Maria Velho da Costa Prize. The summary they made of the book does not even deserve comments, as it is pathetic, but what is important to highlight is the note of disqualification regarding its “Mozambicanness” when they call it Luso-Mozambican. It's not that it's inaccurate, but, for those who know the context, it's a touch naughty. Because finally, following the same logic, Adelino Timóteo should be called Tsonga-Mozambican, Álvaro Taruma Ronga-Mozambican, and Macua-Mozambican for writers from the north, etc., etc. Which doesn't appear to happen. At one point, in the novel, she writes: «Our color was never the dominant one. In colonial times we were not white, we were dressed in monhés, mugs of washed asses, the pejorative term to speak of a son of Goan and Portuguese. In the post-colonial period, I was not black and if, in Lisbon, they took me for Brazilian or Cape Verdean, in France they were lost in thought about where I was from and were surprised when they discovered that I was African. There I had to explain the wanderings of my DNA before settling in Mozambique. It took me a long time to accept that it was like that because yes, and that it was no curse that that was the case; on the contrary, I could even discover intense patterns of fantasy and freedom in this harlequin cloak that is our genealogy…” In modern-day Mozambique, it means being white… and that immediately takes precedence over what you do or what you are. It's sad, but as Naipaul warned, in these parts race is everything and people live as deeply immersed in it as, in other places, in religion. 12/10/22 Bruno Latour, an anthropologist who studied the sciences and their procedures and legitimations, law, technique and religions and who entertained himself by turning his back on the dogmas of the “moderns”, has died. For him, the place, the role of the specialist, was defined more by the perplexity he embodied than by the supposed truth that would sustain him. He proposed, as an alternative to the goodness of multiculturalism and its equivocal cliché “all different, all equal”, a return to “diplomacy”, given that this, precisely, does not start from the assumption that “we are unified because we share the same nature” but simply highlights that we “still do not share a common world”; It is necessary that we deal head-on with the “abyss of disagreement” that separates us, instead of trying to deceive it. And about politics he said, boldly: «If transparency is demanded from politicians, we kill them; if anyone demands fidelity to what the other was or what he says, kill him; If a mimetic relationship is required between what the crowd wants and what its representative says, representation is killed. These must therefore, by definition, betray, deceive, modify, distort the word. Without this ability to bend the word and betray, political language would not exist. I put forward the perfectly plausible hypothesis that political vocabulary will become as inaccessible as the religious language of the last twenty, three or fifty years.» (in “Un Monde Pluriel mais Commun”). I believe we will be needed to decipher this frantically complex world that has fallen to us.

item.title: Fuxi
item.description: O my friends, companions of these disastrous journeys through countries as old as the stars and the Moon, and as strange as the deepest interior of our wretched souls, what surprises still await us, what secret magics will we still have to overcome, what horrors will arise before our pupils dilated with astonishment and terror? Increasingly closer to our precarious existences, howls the monster of war, which threatens to merge humanity once and for all with the earth, incessantly traversed by the squalid specter of hunger and the invisible evil of ten thousand pestilences. Oh damned human, who believes you are superior to Nature itself, but then you reveal yourself, time and time again, incapable of simply ordering your existence and do not understand that you are possessed by fickle anger and are a slave to your own ambition! You are not satisfied with white jade or resplendent gold, there is no treasure that quenches your inextinguishable thirst for command over other men, animals and things; for eternal anguish dwells in you; in you the expelled serpents crawl; for you grows, powerful and impressive, the most fertile ivy of Evil! This is perhaps why on the Mount they call the White Deer, where one of the eight immortals on a sacred mount soared through the heavens towards Penglai, lives the fuxi, a bird whose shape resembles that of a rooster, although topped by a human head. What strange copulations, what monstrous embraces, what terrible procedures must have occurred to make the existence of this sinister animal possible? We don't know, but all it takes is for our exhausted mind to dare to come up with an answer, and then we feel our skin covered in horrendous goosebumps and our heart races in parade, like a wild horse whipped by the night whip of fear, unresponsive to reins and containment. Let men refrain from visiting Monte do Veado, because although there are riches capable of calming the wildest ambitions, if they have the unfortunate luck of catching a glimpse of a fuxi, it is certain that a war will soon break out and the cultivated fields will perish, because of it the tame animals will be beheaded, because of it the best young men of two generations will be sacrificed and the pride of the countries will be destroyed. The fuxi makes a sound reminiscent of its name. If you hear it, dark night or clear day, be on your way, because the path you are on is the surest path to misfortune!

item.title: Procrastinators Club
item.description: While rereading Easter, because of an article I'm preparing, I come across this excerpt, taken from “The Universal Man”: «Each man, morally, is one and absolute; but he has to live with attenuating his personality. (…) Coexistence is only possible between people reduced to a negative or hollow presence, which constantly gives way. Hence, the emptiness of society, an immense ghost made up of countless dead people. A superior man is antisocial or criminal. His destiny is prison and the desert”. I hesitate to think whether Pascoaes reflects here on the political-social conditions that muzzle, and, worse, in a sad country like Portugal in the middle of the Estado Novo (and the book is from 1931) where any possibilities of spontaneity and frankness in human relationships were restricted, or if it merely lends to the behavior of (oppressed) men a metaphysical framework, an “essence”, declared in the presumption of each being, in isolation, “one and absolute”. Sometimes, in addition to an excess of Spirituality (or Saudade), Pascoaes lacks the world. He is a great poet, but he is a poet who takes Ampoules-of-Great-Ideas and who therefore writes often trapped by a programmatic furor, ceasing to have pores; It is not uncommon for a glass to be erected between him and reality, the same glass that made him write in O Bailado, from 1921: «Everything is a ghost. There are only clouds, clouds of voices, clouds of souls, of afflictions and tragedies! Clouds and more clouds, appearances and more appearances! And a divine lightning that pierces them, the instantaneous Apparition that appears and throws us to the ground, dazzled!» Sometimes I felt like reminding him, as António Sérgio did, in the article “Regeneration and Tradition, Moral and Economy”, which he dedicated to Pascoaes: «Pascoais, Pascoais my dear friend: you are a pure, excellent and noble poet, but also a victim of this social environment, like us all: of this horrible isolation that you praise and I curse». «As a child, he stole two blackbirds», says Jacinto do Prado Coelho, he stole them from a nest, and he felt remorse for that all his life. The Person, few more mischiefs are known to him. Only in Álvaro de Campos does a hint of wickedness and obscenity emerge: «Ah, and the ordinary and dirty people, who always look the same,/ Who use swear words as usual words,/ Whose sons steal from grocery store doors/ And whose daughters at the age of eight – and I think this is beautiful and I love it! –/ Decent-looking men masturbate in stairwells.” (Triumphal ode). Both poets, driven by a certain idea of ​​sanctity, had the same inability to break the siege of their skin with the overflow of someone else's dam, the same curse of sublimating the concrete ferocity of eros with the gentleness of the verses. Obviously, they are both “bigger than life”, but sometimes it makes me unnerved to watch, from my position as a hatter at the Procrastinators Club, the tantrums between them, just to see who occupies, in the internal organization, the place of President and Treasurer. Although Pessoa guessed more things, for example, what he wrote in the lesser-known Ode Martial and which is reflected in the situation we live in today, in a different way, with his heart in his hands: «MARTIAL ODE (…) Helahoho! helahoho! The poor widow's sewing machine who was bayoneted... She sewed indefinitely in the afternoon… The table where the old people played, Everything mixed, everything mixed with bodies, with blood, All one river, one wave, one dragging horror. Helahoho! helahoho! I unearthed the child's tin train pressed into the middle of the [road, And I cried like all the mothers in the world about the horror of life. My pantheistic feet tripped over the sewing machine [widow who was killed with a bayonet And that poor instrument of peace stuck a spear in my heart. Yes, I was the one to blame for everything, I was the soldier for them all Who killed, raped, burned and broke, It was me and my shame and my remorse like a shadow [misshapen They travel around the world like Ashavero, But behind my steps there are footsteps the size of infinity And a physical fear of being accountable to God makes me close my eyes. [eyes suddenly. (…) I ordered, captain, to shoot the trembling peasants, I let the daughters of all fathers tied to trees be raped, Now I saw that it was inside my heart that all this happened, And everything scalds and suffocates and I can't move without it [everything be the same. God have mercy on me, I didn't have it from anyone! LÀ-BAS, JE NE SAIS OÙ…” What is clear today, at this disastrous moment in which Cristiano & Companhia failed in their mission in Braga, and in which the self-sabotage of Russia's gas pipelines reminds us that Putin will indeed be willing to do anything to maintain dominance over the areas endorsed, is that, bomb for bomb, the “bomb” of the Fifth Empire was preferable. However, even in this, my dears, we were irresolute, a little more on the side of piela than determined, and we procrastinated.

item.title: Hormuz | Tokyo urges Tehran to cease actions that threaten navigation
item.description: Tokyo on Tuesday urged Tehran to cease actions that threaten navigation in the Strait of Hormuz, as the debate intensifies over international intervention to protect maritime traffic in that fundamental route for energy trade. Japanese Foreign Minister Toshimitsu Motegi made the appeal on Tuesday night during a phone call with his Iranian counterpart, Abbas Araghchi, according to a statement from the head of Japanese diplomacy. “Minister Motegi strongly urged Iran to immediately cease attacks on civilian installations and infrastructure in the Gulf countries, as well as actions that threaten the safety of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz,” the statement revealed. The Japanese minister expressed deep concern about the continuation of the exchange of “retaliatory attacks” since the previous telephone call held on March 9, and with the extent of the damage, which even affected neighboring countries. Furthermore, Motegi expressed concern about the high number of ships linked to Japan that are currently stranded in the Persian Gulf and asked Iran to take the necessary measures to guarantee the safety of all ships in the Strait of Hormuz, “including those from Japan and other Asian countries”. For his part, Araghchi explained Iran's position and both ministers agreed to maintain close communication with a view to quickly easing tensions, according to the Japanese statement. Expectations of a potential deployment of Japanese military ships to the region could be one of the key themes at the summit this Thursday in Washington between Japanese Prime Minister, Sanae Takaichi, and the US President. Asked yesterday about the possibility of sending Japanese Self-Defense Forces personnel to the area, Takaichi reiterated that, for now, there are no plans to do so.

item.title: Tourism | Number of Chinese traveling to Japan fell by 45%
item.description: The number of Chinese tourists in Japan fell 45.2 percent in February, compared to February 2025, despite the Lunar New Year holidays, according to official data published yesterday, in a context of strong tensions between Tokyo and Beijing. The archipelago received 396,400 visitors from mainland China last month, according to the Japanese Tourism Agency, despite the supposedly prosperous Chinese New Year period, which this year fell in February. China is, by far, the largest tourist source market for Japan, representing approximately a quarter of all arrivals in the year until the end of November, but with significant successive drops since then. Relations between the two countries have deteriorated since Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi's statements about Taiwan in the Japanese parliament last November, following which Beijing urged its citizens to avoid any trips to Japan. Takaichi suggested at the time that the country's military intervention would be possible in the event of a Chinese attack on Taiwan, an island that Beijing considers inalienable Chinese territory. In February it announced that it would sanction 40 Japanese companies and organizations accused of participating in the new militarization of Japan, namely prohibiting 20 of them from acquiring goods and technologies with both civil and military potential from companies based in China.

item.title: Japan postpones resumption of operations at the largest plant in the world
item.description: Japanese power company Tokyo Electric Power Company (TEPCO) yesterday postponed the restart of commercial operations at Kashiwazaki-Kariwa, the world's largest nuclear plant by capacity, closed since 2011 after the accident in Fukushima. In a statement, TEPCO said that, last Thursday, an alarm indicated a small electrical leak in reactor number 6, which began transmitting energy on February 9, after 14 years of suspension. After an investigation, the company determined that there was no real leak, but rather a broken conductor that triggered the alarm by mistake, something that “does not directly affect the safety of the reactor”, which remains stable. TEPCO continues to investigate the details of the incident and has not yet announced a new date for the resumption of commercial operations at the reactor. The restart had already been postponed on February 26th, due to problems with the control bar alarms. TEPCO restarted the Kashiwazaki-Kariwa plant on January 21, 15 years after it was closed in 2011 following the Fukushima nuclear accident. This was the first time that TEPCO, which managed the Fukushima plant before the disaster, reactivated one of its plants since the nuclear accident, caused by the strong earthquake and subsequent tsunami, which hit northeastern Japan on March 11, 2011. The accident led to a nuclear blackout in the country, where the majority of nuclear reactors remain deactivated. Status point Currently, 14 reactors are operational, 19 have suspended their operations and 27 are awaiting dismantling, according to data from the International Atomic Energy Agency. Reactors 6 and 7 at Kashiwazaki-Kariwa have already undergone revisions for their respective reactivation in 2017, but the plant was subsequently ordered to remain shut down due to security gaps against terrorist attacks. In December 2023, the measures adopted were approved and, since then, TEPCO has been going through the necessary procedures to put both reactors into operation. With a capacity of more than eight thousand megawatts, the Kashiwazaki-Kariwa plant is a fundamental piece in TEPCO's energy supply plan and is in line with the strategy promoted by the Japanese Government of Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi to boost nuclear plants and meet emissions reduction targets.

item.title: Chinese special envoy to the Middle East puts Palestinian issue on the agenda
item.description: The Chinese special envoy for the Middle East, Zhai Jun, stressed yesterday, in Cairo, to the Egyptian Minister of Foreign Affairs, Badr Abdelatty, that the Palestinian issue “must not be ignored or forgotten again”. Zhai indicated that the “Palestinian issue is fundamental to long-term peace, stability and security in the Middle East” and that agreements following the war in Gaza “must be in line with the implementation of the ‘two-state solution'”, according to a statement published yesterday by the Chinese Ministry of Foreign Affairs. The Chinese diplomat declared that “a further spread of the conflict would cause greater losses, affecting regional economic development and global energy security”, underlining the “urgency” of a “ceasefire” to “return to the correct path of resolving disputes through diplomatic channels”. Abdelatty stated that “the current escalation of regional tensions has serious consequences and does not benefit either party”, urging Iran to “cease attacks against Arab countries”. “The current conflict must not divert international attention from the Palestinian issue, nor from the ceasefire agreement in Gaza, as well as post-war governance and reconstruction agreements,” said the Egyptian diplomat, according to the Chinese statement. According to Abdelatty, “Egypt and China share a high degree of consensus on regional issues, and Egypt is willing to strengthen coordination and cooperation with China to jointly promote the re-establishment of peace and stability in the region”. On the ground Zhai, whose agenda of meetings in the Middle East countries was not detailed, is in the region carrying out “active mediation” in the conflict, as part of which he has planned contacts with the parties involved to promote the return to negotiations, the Chinese Ministry of Foreign Affairs recently reported. The diplomat has already visited other countries such as Saudi Arabia, the United Arab Emirates or Bahrain, where he defended a ceasefire and “respect” for the “sovereignty, security and territorial integrity of the Arab States in the Gulf region”. The conflict has pitted Iran against the United States and Israel since the end of February, when both countries launched attacks against Iranian territory, to which Tehran responded with offensives against several Gulf countries and positions linked to Washington in the region.

item.title: Profit of digital giant Tencent rises 16 percent to 28,311 ME in 2025
item.description: Chinese technology company Tencent recorded a net profit of 224,842 million yuan in 2025, which represents growth of 16 percent compared to the previous year. In the results sent yesterday to the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, where it is listed, the company also indicated that revenues increased 14 percent year-on-year, to around 751.766 million yuan. The technology company highlighted “healthy growth” throughout the year, driven by the improvement of its capabilities in artificial intelligence (AI), which allowed it to optimize advertising and increase the number of users of its video games, as well as the growth of its cloud computing business, both in revenue and profits. With regard to AI, an area in which both Tencent and its competitors are investing heavily, the company highlighted the hiring of “top talent”, the creation of processes to consolidate its models and the application of these capabilities to products such as the Yuanbao chatbot or the WeChat social network. “The growing capacity of our large-scale language model HY 3.0, as well as the usefulness of AI products such as Yuanbao, WorkBuddy or QClaw, constitute encouraging initial signs that these investments will generate new opportunities”, said the company, also alluding to the increase in the amounts allocated to its development, supported by the performance of the main businesses. “The demand for consumption and entertainment, as well as the satisfaction associated with creation and productivity, allows Tencent to offer AI services that improve its users’ capabilities in these areas,” he added. Above expected The WeChat social network ended the year with around 1.418 million monthly active users, 33 million more than at the end of 2024. In the last quarter, Tencent recorded a 14 percent increase in profits compared to the same period last year, exceeding analysts' forecasts, which pointed to a 7 percent increase. Revenues grew 13 percent, in line with expectations. In a separate statement, the Board of Directors proposed the payment of a dividend of 5.3 Hong Kong dollars per share, for the 2025 financial year. Tencent, the largest Chinese company by market capitalization and one of the 20 largest in the world, is known for developing WeChat and for being the largest global distributor of video games, with titles such as “League of Legends”.

item.title: Hong Kong | Appeals from seven convicted of storming Parliament in 2019 rejected
item.description: The Hong Kong Court of Appeal upheld the measures only applied in the first instance to protesters who, in 2019, invaded Parliament, causing damage and displaying the British flag. The Hong Kong court yesterday rejected the appeals presented by seven protesters convicted of riots that occurred during the invasion of Parliament in 2019, at the height of the protests that shook the region. Among the protesters who filed an appeal was actor Gregory Wong Chung-yiu, who was contesting his conviction for public disorder and a sentence of six years and two months in prison. The Hong Kong Court of Appeal upheld the decision, unanimously concluding that the involvement in the invasion of the Legislative Council was deliberate and not that of an “innocent bystander”. “Knowing that the complex was occupied, he dressed in black and entered the chamber, where he not only handed over material to journalists, but also maintained physical contact with the protesters, hugging them,” the sentence states. The judges also rejected the claim that the sentence imposed on Gregory Wong was disproportionate. The court also rejected the appeals of six other defendants. Five of them – including former student leader Althea Suen Hiu-nam, who pleaded guilty – only appealed their sentences, which included sentences of up to six years and ten months in prison. The sixth defendant, Amy Pat Wai-fun, contested both the conviction and the sentence of four years and nine months imposed. In February 2024, a lower court judge sentenced 11 defendants to prison terms ranging from four and a half years to six years and ten months. Two journalists, tried together, were fined up to 1,500 Hong Kong dollars for entering or remaining in the complex, although they were not charged with rioting. In its 2024 ruling, the court emphasized the symbolic nature of the attack, stating that participants “torn up copies of the Basic Law [Hong Kong’s ‘mini’ constitution] and displayed colonial flags,” gestures it deemed “provocative and defiant of authority.” The court also highlighted that the episode “undermined governance capacity and left deep scars on Hong Kong society”. Law and order The events took place on the night of July 1, 2019, on the 22nd anniversary of the transfer of sovereignty from the United Kingdom to China. Hundreds of protesters, wearing helmets and masks, stormed the complex, caused damage and displayed the British colonial flag before being dispersed with tear gas. The invasion was one of the most tense moments of the protests against the extradition law to mainland China. The demonstrations, with millions of participants and demands for universal suffrage, culminated in the imposition, by the Chinese central government, of the National Security Law in 2020.

item.title: Aviation | UAE reopens airspace after Iranian attacks
item.description: The United Arab Emirates (UAE) announced yesterday morning the return of air traffic to normal operations, after having completely and temporarily closed its airspace overnight due to Iranian attacks against its territory. In a statement from the Emirates General Civil Aviation Authority, the entity explained that given the stabilization of the situation and the end of previously established precautionary measures, the decision was taken to resume flights. "The Authority confirms that this decision comes after a full assessment of safety conditions, in coordination with the relevant authorities. We emphasize that constant monitoring will continue to be carried out to ensure the highest levels of aviation safety", added the note. The closure announced overnight was described as “an exceptional precautionary measure” to guarantee the safety of flights and protect UAE territory, coinciding with the Ministry of Defense’s announcement that the country’s air defenses were responding to missile and drone threats from Iran. The decision to close the airspace came just hours after flights to and from Dubai International Airport began to gradually resume, following a temporary suspension applied in the early hours of Monday. This suspension was motivated by an incident with a drone that caused a fire near the infrastructure, without causing any casualties. The joint attack by the United States and Israel against Iran, on February 28, triggered a war with strong repercussions for the Persian Gulf countries, which became the target of Iranian attacks against American bases and interests in the region. Since the start of the conflict, flights to or from the UAE have been subject to suspensions.

item.title: East Timor | Government guarantees fuel and can intervene in prices
item.description: The Minister of Petroleum and Mineral Resources of Timor-Leste, Francisco Monteiro, yesterday guaranteed fuel supplies for the next two months and admitted the Government's intervention if prices soar due to the conflict in the Middle East. “So far, we are not at a critical stage when it comes to solutions, but the security of fuel supply remains stable and assured for the next two months”, said Francisco Monteiro, at a press conference. The minister was speaking to journalists after the rise in fuel prices in Timor-Leste, following the conflict in the Middle East, and after criticism from the political opposition, which accused the Government of not providing clarifications to the population. “The Ministry of Petroleum assures the public that energy security, in terms of fuel reserves, is assured for the coming months”, insisted the government official. The minister also explained that there is “very intensive coordination” with the companies that supply fuel to the country and last week a meeting was held to “openly discuss what each entity is doing and plans to do in the near future”. Regarding the increase in fuel prices in the country, Francisco Monteiro explained that Timor-Leste adopted the free market and that its cost will always be affected by the world market. The minister highlighted that the Government may adopt measures if the price of oil continues to rise. “Appropriate measures will be taken to minimize, as much as possible, the negative impacts on the economy if prices increase significantly,” he said. Francisco Monteiro added that there is no reason for alarm and that there is coordination between all entities involved. "We must maintain confidence. Regarding the issue of prices, we are monitoring the situation and, in the short term, the Government should adopt some measures to minimize the impacts as much as possible", he added.

item.title: Middle East | Beijing sends aid to alleviate “profound humanitarian disaster”
item.description: China assured yesterday that “the current hostilities have caused a profound humanitarian disaster” in the countries of the Middle East, to which it proposed sending emergency aid, also conveying solidarity with the affected populations. Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Lin Jian declared, at a press conference, that “China has always put into practice the concept of a community with a shared future for humanity, defending the spirit of internationalism and humanitarianism”. Lin announced that the country “has decided to provide emergency humanitarian assistance to Iran, Jordan, Lebanon and Iraq, in the hope that this will help alleviate the difficult humanitarian situation faced by local populations”, without detailing the scope of the aid. “China will continue to do everything in its power to promote peace, facilitate the rapid restoration of regional peace and stability, and prevent the humanitarian crisis from worsening further,” the spokesperson added. Lin was responding to a question about recent estimates by the UN Refugee Agency (UNHCR), which estimated the number of people displaced within Iran due to the current conflict with Israel and the United States at 3.2 million. In Lebanon, the conflict between Israel and the Lebanese Shiite movement Hezbollah, supported by Iran, has caused the forced displacement of more than 600,000 people, according to the UNHCR. China, Tehran's main trading partner and biggest buyer of its oil, has repeatedly condemned attacks on Iran by the United States and Israel for “violating the sovereignty” of the Persian country. The Asian country also urged the protection of maritime routes, taking into account that 45 percent of the oil it imports passes through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Miguel Carvalho, author of the book “Por Dentro do Chega”: “Chega attracted everything and a pair of boots”
item.description:
Journalist Miguel Carvalho has long been investigating Chega, the far-right political party founded by André Ventura. Guest of the “Rota das Letras” Literary Festival, Miguel Carvalho comes to Macau to talk about the book “Por Dentro do Chega – A Face Oculta da Extrema-Rireita em Portugal” and the growth of populism He will share the session “From Mistake to Populism” with another journalist, João Miguel Tavares, who wrote a work about José Sócrates. What parallels do you find between Sócrates and Chega? I find it quite curious that the debate between these two books [“José Sócrates – Ascensão (1957-2005)” and “Por Dentro do Chega”] took place. This is because André Ventura himself assumed, although he later denied it, but it is recorded, that he voted for José Sócrates in the first term. I describe in the book the exemplary way in which Ventura, in some way, looks at himself in relation to his own political persona, in the positive characteristics he saw in José Sócrates, in relation to the idea of ​​the politician who came to change everything and anything. But José Sócrates is a hated figure within Chega, he is, perhaps, the politician that Chega refers to most to identify the bad guys in politics. In many cases, Sócrates was the motivation why many joined Chega. In my research I found many activists who voted for José Sócrates, believing it to be the best thing that could happen to Portuguese politics. The book is in its ninth edition in Portugal. Did you expect this reaction from the public, taking into account the growth of Chega's electorate? There is a dimension to this that, for me, is a little unreal. We are talking about a country like Portugal, which has marginal reading rates, and we already have 36 thousand copies in circulation in total. But this reaction from Chega's electoral universe, both from people who have already left or who are still there, is not a complete surprise to me. I received a lot of messages via email or Facebook from people who had either left Chega or were still there, but who respected my journalistic work, regardless of what they might think of me as a citizen or my convictions. People who were reading the book with a critical mind. I know people who read the book and left Chega, and others who continued. The book is 90 percent Talking About Itself, and I'm grateful that people continue to respect my journalistic work, regardless of their opinion of me.
Chega is the result of a wave of populism that has emerged all over the world. Have we learned nothing from history? Not that, and of course at different rates. I dedicate the last chapter to the entry of extremist narratives into schools and colleges, and how the Chega narrative is contaminating, and making complicit, a generation that has a great distance from the 25th of April. Based on testimonies from teachers and other professionals, I report the increasingly frequent presence of students in classrooms giving the Nazi salute, some who quoted parts of “Mein Kampf”, or adopted simplistic narratives, most of them completely false and based on the misinformation that is widespread. These are young people who are bringing things to the classroom that we thought were already resolved. But this also happens because something failed at school, and perhaps Portugal is not unique in this regard. Perhaps, in part, it justifies the rise of these narratives and movements. I also know, from access to Chega's vast internal documentation over the years, that teachers are one of the professional classes with the greatest representation in Chega's activism, from basic education to academia. Here we have factors that are generating a very worrying situation in relation to the entry of extremist ideas into a universe that, in many cases, still does not even vote. One of the ideas that comes out of your book is that we cannot talk about a Chega-type voter. It's not easy for me [to make this definition]. I don't know, at the moment, who the typical Chega voter is because it varies greatly depending on geographic location and political orientations. In other words, Chega attracted everything and a pair of boots, so to speak. Initially, he sought people from the parties that were closest to him ideologically, namely the PSD and the CDS. I went a lot into the field, interviewed hundreds of them, and it is now verifiable with studies carried out on electoral behavior that Chega managed to attract people from various ideological options in the past, from the PCP to the Left Bloc. There were many traditional Socialist Party voters who switched to Chega. The party is disruptive even in relation to the traditional party system itself. In what sense? I believe that a voter or a member of one of the traditional parties may even disagree with the leader at a certain point or disagree with the program at a certain stage, but if he is in that party and votes for that party, it is because he is minimally comfortable, let's say, 70 or 80 percent, with its ideas. Now, Chega doesn't work like that, and has come to break with all of this. We have to imagine Chega as a kind of restaurant where there is chef Ventura who invents about ten dishes, with those ideas that we know by heart and stir-fry, about immigration, the gypsy community, the chemical castration of pedophiles, and then this electoral universe chooses three or four dishes that are most relevant to them, depending on their personal and professional journey, or their frustrations, or their community, even. For example, it's easy to find people from Chega who don't even agree with André Ventura's speech about the gypsy community, but they agree when he justifies the need for there to be no sexual education in schools, because that would only serve to teach children how to masturbate. Then they already believe and think it is a fight worth fighting. Each case is different and I have never encountered a party so atomized and individualized at that level. A few years ago, for Visão magazine, he carried out an investigation into Macanese Vitório Cardoso, linked to the PSD, but seen at Chega demonstrations. Is this an example of someone who approached Chega from a traditional party? Everything I know about him and his public statements, some of which, however, have been deleted, and everything I have read over the years about the public expression of his political thought, makes him obviously a figure closer to Chega than to the PSD, even though this PSD is what it is, without much ideological support. It wouldn't surprise me, therefore, if people like Vitório felt much more comfortable in a party like Chega than in the one where they supposedly join. In the Portuguese diaspora, Chega has also grown. How do you explain this phenomenon, taking into account that we are talking about a party with an anti-immigration discourse? This side is less covered in the book, because at the time there were no conditions to delve deeper into the topic. Chega has, from the outset, seen a great mobilization on the part of some Portuguese businesspeople in the diaspora. In France and Switzerland this is very evident. There are, therefore, big businessmen who put themselves at the service of the party and André Ventura, using their resources to get as many people as possible to vote. Chega denied it, saying that it has nothing to do with these organizations, and that they are individual initiatives, but the truth is that even in the legislative elections in Geneva, Chega was the only party that managed to put electoral propaganda in the mailboxes. This reflects another aspect, which is the fact that the PS and PSD have been comfortable, in some countries, with the immigrant vote for too long. In other words, as they usually divided voters among themselves, they did not worry much about the political and media literacy of a vast immigration sector. It is necessary to remember that 90 percent of Portuguese people living in the most important countries for Portuguese immigration did not go to vote, and this is often justified by the fact that they feel that Portugal has forgotten them. And then there is another issue, which is the fact that even though people emigrated in very difficult circumstances to escape poverty, the truth is that with their work they achieved a certain status and today consider that any immigration, even from their Portuguese brothers, is harmful. I noticed this a lot in the Azores, for example, where family and friendship conflicts begin to generate. This, from a psychological point of view, is quite complex to dismantle. Representative José Pereira Coutinho, who won the last legislative elections in Macau, compared himself to André Ventura. Is the president of Chega seen as a kind of political star who almost serves as a reference for others? Yes. It is already clear that the party would have many difficulties in maintaining the political strength it has today without André Ventura, who is someone who has dominated media language for many years, long before Chega. He has an overwhelming command of communication and knows how to simplify, obviously behind many lies and falsehoods. I have been writing this since 2020, Chega is, essentially, a project of personal power. Ventura subjected Chega to the leader's cult, and the party is today, without a doubt, less free than it was at the beginning. With less internal criticism? There were, despite everything, certain currents and people with their own thoughts, who, whether or not they agreed with most of the proposals that the party offered, were capable of taking the stage at a congress and criticizing the leadership. I have serious doubts that this will happen at the next Chega congress. The rulings of the Constitutional Court are overwhelming regarding the lack of internal democracy, and [this happens] out of survival instinct, as André Ventura rarely allowed to give [the stage] to other people. There are recent studies that demonstrate that André Ventura transformed Chega into a leadership very similar to that of the North Korean regime. You can ask me, if he leaves tomorrow, obviously Chega will remain. It suffers a great shock, but we still have the big question, just as we had in the United States: it was not because Donald Trump was out of the White House for four years that he ceased to exist. Here we have the same problem, it will not be because Ventura leaves that “Ventura Country” will cease to exist, it is necessary to respond to the desires and frustrations that generated this “Ventura Country”. If this is not done, this “Ventura Country” will go looking for another Ventura. Will Ventura be prime minister in Portugal in the next legislative period? With or without an absolute majority? If there is a political crisis again, I am convinced that Chega has a high chance of becoming a Government. But the truth is that there are signs of wear and tear in Chega’s narrative. From the electorate that joined in strongly from the beginning, there are signs that it found other types of motivations. Between the local elections and the first round of the presidential elections, and this was not talked about much, Chega lost around 100 thousand votes. These are very different elections, but 100,000 votes are not lost like that either. And the truth is that Chega had, in relation to the last local councils, very high expectations which, even with three municipal councils won, were not confirmed. The truth is that, if there is no immediate change in the political cycle, Chega will have more difficulty reaching the Government.

item.title: Tracy Choi’s new film “Girlfriends” premieres today at MGM Macau: “It’s very difficult to be a director in Macau”
item.description: In “Girlfriends”, her new film and fourth feature film of her career, Tracy Choi created a kind of alter-ego, through a character who, after studying in Taiwan, lives in Hong Kong while struggling to finance a film. In an interview with HM, the director says she wants to continue making films about women and their experiences. The official premiere of “Girlfriends” is scheduled for today, at 7:30 pm, at MGM Macau It's been exactly ten years since “Sisterhood” premiered, and Tracy Choi probably wouldn't have imagined that, a decade later, the success would be real, with more and more international recognition for her way of making films. After placing a Macau film at the Busan International Film Festival for the first time, with “Girlfriends”, it is the local director’s turn to bring her new film, the fourth feature film of her career, to your home. The premiere takes place today at the “Treasure Box” room at MGM Macau, starting at 7:30 pm. We spoke to the director before the premiere of the new work, who told us how she started thinking about the film's script during the Covid-19 period. "The story is about a girl who grew up in Macau, who studied in Taiwan and went to work in Hong Kong. I thought about it [the film] during the pandemic, because we couldn't go anywhere and we had nothing to do. I decided to write a script about myself: a girl from Macau growing up, seeing how she grows up and how she finds herself, something like that." The cast is made up of actresses that Tracy Choi has worked with, namely Fish Liew, Jennifer Yu, Elizabeth Tang, Ning Han, Natalie Hsu and Eliz Lao. "One of them has already received several nominations and even awards in Hong Kong, including best actress [Jennifer Yu]. This time, after almost ten years, we worked together again. I think we grew a lot and it was like working among friends. It was a great happiness to be able to work with someone I know well. The chemistry between them is also very good, because they are friends in real life." In the case of Natalie Hsu, she was nominated for the “Best Actress” award at the 43rd edition of the Hong Kong Film Awards, while Eliz Lao is a local actress, who even had a film cycle under her own name at Cinemateca Paixão. Eliz Lao made films in Hong Kong and China, having experience in television and theater, without forgetting her training in ballet. Regarding today's premiere, Tracy Choi highlights that “films with LGBT themes, or stories about female growth, are not very commercial”. "Whenever we tried to enter festivals, we were told that the film would not have great sales. Still, even if the film does not have great commercial success, we hope to reach more audiences, and make more people aware of the film", he highlighted. “Girlfriends” was shot and produced with public funds, and filmed in 2024. Tracy Choi and her team also sought investment in Taiwan and Hong Kong. After launching in Macau, the team is preparing for new debuts in the neighboring region and in Taiwan. Regarding the recent experience in Busan, South Korea, with “Girlfriends” as part of the “Vision Section” competition section, Tracy Choi brought back the best memories. "It was a great experience to premiere the film there. It was the first Macau film to be shown at the festival, we had a great night. The public also seemed to like it and we ended up being invited to go to other festivals, and that was important for the whole team." Break through barriers Tracy Choi's cinema reflects a little on Macau's imagination and women's experiences, but also explores emotional scenarios of feelings of belonging, memory and memories. "When it comes to women and stories from Macao, we are always represented by others, like films from Hong Kong, or films from China. We are often represented by other directors and there are not many voices from local directors, especially when it comes to women. So, as I am a woman, I really hope to focus on how women grow up here or in other places in Asia, on what women face. In my next projects I hope to continue to focus on the ways women face the world in different situations," she said. Tracy Choi makes no secret of the fact that “Girlfriends” has an openly LGBT theme, whereas, in her previous films, it wasn’t so much like that. "Maybe they were more about Macau or [the feeling] of sisterhood, something like that. But in 'Girlfriends' we mainly focused on an LGBT love story. I hope it's a little more commercial for an LGBT film, and the way we chose to tell the story is more commercial. I hope that the general public, and not just LGBT, can also feel love for LGBT people and identify with that kind of story." In the case of “Sisterhood”, her first feature film, there are references to the transition of Macau, in 1999, and two former masseuses, Sei and Ling, who spent good times together. However, years later, one of them realizes the other's big secret, in a friendship that takes on different forms. The difficulties experienced Despite the success she has experienced, Tracy Choi highlights that “it is very difficult to be a director in Macau because there are not many productions”. "But it's not just me, other filmmakers in Macau are also working hard to make things happen. We look for other ways [to support ourselves]: I teach and work as a producer to make other productions come to fruition. We hope, all together, to be able to gain more audience and attention, so that the next directors, and productions in Macau, have a slightly easier path", he added. The director is still harvesting the laurels of her fourth film, but is already working on the post-production of another feature film. “Last year I also filmed another production, in co-production with China, and we hope to finish before the summer and show the film at the end of this year.” Tracy Choi was born in 1988 and studied Film Production at Shih Hsin University, having grown up in the San Kiu neighborhood. “Sisterhood” was his presentation film that gained him some notoriety, particularly after receiving the Audience Choice Award at the Macau International Film Festival. He was only 28 years old at the time. About this moment, he recalled to Revista Macau: "After graduating in Taiwan, I returned to Macau to work. At that time, about ten years ago, there was no cinema in Macau. There were no local productions being filmed regularly. That didn't happen and we took advantage of the opportunity given to us. I challenged my friends to join the competition organized by the Cultural Center, obtain financing and film something that had an important meaning for us. It was also very important that my first feature film could premiere at the Cultural Center, because it was something that brought visibility to the local film industry”, he said.

item.title: Marcelo Loureiro, author and teacher: “The indigenato was a continuation of slavery”
item.description: He was recently in Macau to present the book “Colonial Citizenship: law, race and rights through the Portuguese empire”. Marcelo Loureiro's work explores the way in which, in the Portuguese empire, Portuguese citizenship was shaped according to skin color. Macau was no exception, although with different contours compared to Africa, argues the professor at the University of Leicester Image: Rui Cunha Foundation How did this investigation begin and how did the themes of colonialism and citizenship arise? Everything was born from a personal and family experience. I grew up in Rio de Janeiro, in a family marked by the Portuguese diaspora on the one hand, and colonial silence on the other. My paternal grandmother, Portuguese, kept the documents that allowed me, at the age of thirteen, to claim Portuguese citizenship in the back of a closet. My other grandmother, mestizo, cafuza, had no papers, no stories to tell; From her to me, there was only silence. This division, between those who have the right to documents and a voice and those who are forced to be erased, was the starting point for questioning the legal construction of citizenship and nationality. When I arrived in Portugal to study Law in Coimbra, I began to realize that the law that recognized me as Portuguese was the same one that historically denied this recognition to millions of people based on race, 'civilization' and skin color. The book is, in part, an attempt to understand this legal structure of inclusion and exclusion, it is a denunciation of the injustice behind rights.
